({{ill|Shinhodaka Onsen|ja|新穂高温泉|12=draft}})]] From a management perspective, especially with a free-flowing hot spring of high source temperature, weather changes can significantly alter the bath’s temperature, requiring more precise temperature control. Additionally, due to the constant possibility of contamination by {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ|12=draft}} bacteria, stricter hygiene measures than those for indoor baths are neededJapan Association of Living and Environmental Health “[http://www.seiei.or.jp/db-cho/le-11.html Legionella Prevention Guidelines]” (Revised March 1999; accessed January 5, 2016).. == Other == [[File:Iceland - Blue Lagoon 09 (6571266721).jpg|thumb|{{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft}} ({{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft}})]] * The open-air bath “Edo-jōwa Yoichi” at {{ill|Spa Resort Hawaiians|ja|スパリゾートハワイアンズ|12=draft}} in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}} is listed in the “{{ill|Guinness World Records|ja|ギネス世界記録|12=draft}} 2009” as the world’s largest open-air bath with a total tub area of 1000m²{{Cite web|和書|url=http://blog.hawaiians.co.jp/archives/50736632.html|title=世界一の大露天風呂|publisher=スパリゾートハワイアンズ|date=2008-11-08|accessdate=2021-06-03}}. * In {{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft}}, there is an open-air spa called the {{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft}} that covers about 5,000m² (with approximately 2,400m² open for general bathing)Iceland Tourism & Culture Research Institute “[http://www.iceland-kankobunka.jp/guide/reykjanes/blgn1.htm Tourist Spot Guide: Blue Lagoon]” (accessed January 5, 2016).. * {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|12=draft}} is “Open-Air Bath Day” (a pun on “Ro (6) Ten (.) Bu (2) Ro (6)”). It was established on June 26, 1987, by {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|12=draft}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}Conceived in 1987 as part of a local revitalization project by young people in Yubara Onsen.[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html Official site of the 6.26 Open-Air Bath Day Committee] (Yubara Town Ryokan Cooperative). == Notes == == Sources == == Related topics == * {{ill|part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburoでの組織的盗撮事件|12=draft|1=Organized voyeurism case in an open-air bath|3=露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件}} == External links == * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm Open-Air Bath Banzuke Chart] {{Onsen-stub}} '''Rotenburo''' (露天風呂) {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs]] [[Category:Bathing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:露天風呂]] [[ja:%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82]] [[ko:%EB%85%B8%EC%B2%9C%ED%83%95]] [[sn:Rotenburo]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rotenburo}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|日帰り入浴施設|" to "|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-use_bath_facility|"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1={{ill|Sotoyu|ja|外湯|12=draft}} refers to communal baths outside lodging facilities shared by multiple users, {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|12=draft}} denotes in-house hot spring baths privately installed within lodging facilities, and {{ill|Rotenburo|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|12=draft}} describes open-air baths typically without a roof or full enclosure.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:41, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Onsen in Nachikatsuura, Japan.jpg|thumb|280px|An open-air bath overlooking the sea ({{ill|Nanki-Katsuura Onsen|ja|南紀勝浦温泉|12=draft}})]] [[File:Iwojima Higashi onsen.jpg|280px|thumb|A self-flowing hot spring “{{ill|Higashi Onsen (Iōjima)|ja|東温泉|12=draft}}” facing the East China Sea ({{ill|Satsuma Iōjima|ja|硫黄島 (鹿児島県)|12=draft}})]] '''Rotenburo''' (open-air bath) is a type of {{ill|Bath|ja|風呂|12=draft}} built outdoors with no roof or enclosure[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82-663841 Kotobank - Digital Daijisen (Sanseidō)]. It is also called '''Noburo''' (野風呂) or '''Notenburo''' (野天風呂)When referred to as 野天風呂 (or 野風呂), it may mean a bath that is not entirely in its natural state like a {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|12=draft}} but lacks as much development in walls or roofing as a typical open-air bath.. == Overview == Open-air baths can be considered the original “landscape” of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|12=draft}} bathing. They began as naturally emerging hot springs like {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|12=draft}}, in which a simple bathing area (bathtub) was arranged. Rotenburo (open-air baths) are highly popular at hot spring resorts. Reasons include being able to enjoy scenic views while bathing, experiencing a sense of openness, and having less risk of overheating (since heat does not accumulate as much as in an indoor setting). As a result, many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|旅館|12=draft}} and {{ill|Day-use hot spring facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-use_bath_facility|12=draft}} offer open-air baths in addition to {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|12=draft}}. In recent years, not only traditional onsen but also many {{ill|Supersento|ja|スーパー銭湯|12=draft}} have installed open-air baths. Up through the early 1980s, indoor styles such as {{ill|Roman bath|ja|ローマ風呂|12=draft}} or {{ill|Jungle bath|ja|ジャングル風呂|12=draft}} were mainstream. However, around the late 1980s during the {{ill|Japanese asset price bubble|ja|バブル景気|12=draft}}, rotenburo began to draw more attention. Sometimes, even a bath that has a ceiling and three walls (thus feeling nearly as enclosed as an indoor bath) might be marketed as a “rotenburo,” or described as “semi-open-air” (“半露天”). == Rotenburo Banzuke == '''Rotenburo Banzuke''' (露天風呂番付) is a form of {{ill|Onsen banzuke|ja|温泉番付|12=draft}}, ranking hot spring areas that are famous for their open-air baths. It was compiled in 1981, edited by '''Noguchi Fuyuto'''. In this ranking, the Western {{ill|Yokozuna|ja|横綱|12=draft}} is the “Sunayu” at {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|12=draft}}, and the Eastern Yokozuna is {{ill|Takaragawa Onsen|ja|宝川温泉|12=draft}}. At Yubara Onsen, there is a large monument depicting this Rotenburo Banzuke, serving as a symbol of the hot spring resort. == Variations of Open-Air Baths == Yumura onsen20n4592.jpg|Open-air bath in a forest setting
({{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|12=draft}}) Ryokukeitouen Kirishima City Kagoshima Pref09n4592.jpg|Open-air bath at a mountain stream
({{ill|Sakaenoo Onsen|ja|栄之尾温泉|12=draft}}) 190714 Settuskyo Onsen Takatsuki Osaka pref Japan01s3.jpg|Open-air bath with a gorge view
({{ill|Settsukyō Onsen|ja|摂津峡温泉|12=draft}}) Alpa Komakusa 2008.jpg|Open-air bath with a lake view (Tazawako Kōgen Onsen “Alpa Komakusa”) Tamatsukuri onsen yado01s3648.jpg|A guest room with an attached open-air bath
({{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉|12=draft}}) Koeda-Fudousan Katsura Koeda P6237036.JPG|A personal open-air bath built on private villa grounds == Management == [[File:新穂高の湯001.jpg|thumb|Cleaning an open-air bath
({{ill|Shinhodaka Onsen|ja|新穂高温泉|12=draft}})]] From a management perspective, especially with a free-flowing hot spring of high source temperature, weather changes can significantly alter the bath’s temperature, requiring more precise temperature control. Additionally, due to the constant possibility of contamination by {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ|12=draft}} bacteria, stricter hygiene measures than those for indoor baths are neededJapan Association of Living and Environmental Health “[http://www.seiei.or.jp/db-cho/le-11.html Legionella Prevention Guidelines]” (Revised March 1999; accessed January 5, 2016).. == Other == [[File:Iceland - Blue Lagoon 09 (6571266721).jpg|thumb|{{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft}} ({{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft}})]] * The open-air bath “Edo-jōwa Yoichi” at {{ill|Spa Resort Hawaiians|ja|スパリゾートハワイアンズ|12=draft}} in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}} is listed in the “{{ill|Guinness World Records|ja|ギネス世界記録|12=draft}} 2009” as the world’s largest open-air bath with a total tub area of 1000m²{{Cite web|和書|url=http://blog.hawaiians.co.jp/archives/50736632.html|title=世界一の大露天風呂|publisher=スパリゾートハワイアンズ|date=2008-11-08|accessdate=2021-06-03}}. * In {{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft}}, there is an open-air spa called the {{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft}} that covers about 5,000m² (with approximately 2,400m² open for general bathing)Iceland Tourism & Culture Research Institute “[http://www.iceland-kankobunka.jp/guide/reykjanes/blgn1.htm Tourist Spot Guide: Blue Lagoon]” (accessed January 5, 2016).. * {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|12=draft}} is “Open-Air Bath Day” (a pun on “Ro (6) Ten (.) Bu (2) Ro (6)”). It was established on June 26, 1987, by {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|12=draft}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}Conceived in 1987 as part of a local revitalization project by young people in Yubara Onsen.[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html Official site of the 6.26 Open-Air Bath Day Committee] (Yubara Town Ryokan Cooperative). == Notes == == Sources == == Related topics == * {{ill|part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburoでの組織的盗撮事件|12=draft|1=Organized voyeurism case in an open-air bath|3=露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件}} == External links == * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm Open-Air Bath Banzuke Chart] {{Onsen-stub}} '''Rotenburo''' (露天風呂) {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs]] [[Category:Bathing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:露天風呂]] [[ja:%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82]] [[ko:%EB%85%B8%EC%B2%9C%ED%83%95]] [[sn:Rotenburo]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rotenburo}}

Additionally, due to the constant possibility of contamination by {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ|12=draft}} bacteria, stricter hygiene measures than those for indoor baths are neededJapan Association of Living and Environmental Health “[http://www.seiei.or.jp/db-cho/le-11.html Legionella Prevention Guidelines]” (Revised March 1999; accessed January 5, 2016).. == Other == [[File:Iceland - Blue Lagoon 09 (6571266721).jpg|thumb|{{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft}} ({{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft}})]] * The open-air bath “Edo-jōwa Yoichi” at {{ill|Spa Resort Hawaiians|ja|スパリゾートハワイアンズ|12=draft}} in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}} is listed in the “{{ill|Guinness World Records|ja|ギネス世界記録|12=draft}} 2009” as the world’s largest open-air bath with a total tub area of 1000m²{{Cite web|和書|url=http://blog.hawaiians.co.jp/archives/50736632.html|title=世界一の大露天風呂|publisher=スパリゾートハワイアンズ|date=2008-11-08|accessdate=2021-06-03}}. * In {{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft}}, there is an open-air spa called the {{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft}} that covers about 5,000m² (with approximately 2,400m² open for general bathing)Iceland Tourism & Culture Research Institute “[http://www.iceland-kankobunka.jp/guide/reykjanes/blgn1.htm Tourist Spot Guide: Blue Lagoon]” (accessed January 5, 2016).. * {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|12=draft}} is “Open-Air Bath Day” (a pun on “Ro (6) Ten (.) Bu (2) Ro (6)”). It was established on June 26, 1987, by {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|12=draft}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}Conceived in 1987 as part of a local revitalization project by young people in Yubara Onsen.[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html Official site of the 6.26 Open-Air Bath Day Committee] (Yubara Town Ryokan Cooperative). == Notes == == Sources == == Related topics == * {{ill|part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburoでの組織的盗撮事件|12=draft|1=Organized voyeurism case in an open-air bath|3=露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件}} == External links == * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm Open-Air Bath Banzuke Chart] {{Onsen-stub}} '''Rotenburo''' (露天風呂) {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs]] [[Category:Bathing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:露天風呂]] [[ja:%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82]] [[ko:%EB%85%B8%EC%B2%9C%ED%83%95]] [[sn:Rotenburo]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rotenburo}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Day-trip bathing facilities" to "Day-trip bathing facility"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1={{ill|Sotoyu|ja|外湯|12=draft}} refers to communal baths outside lodging facilities shared by multiple users, {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|12=draft}} denotes in-house hot spring baths privately installed within lodging facilities, and {{ill|Rotenburo|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|12=draft}} describes open-air baths typically without a roof or full enclosure.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:41, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Onsen in Nachikatsuura, Japan.jpg|thumb|280px|An open-air bath overlooking the sea ({{ill|Nanki-Katsuura Onsen|ja|南紀勝浦温泉|12=draft}})]] [[File:Iwojima Higashi onsen.jpg|280px|thumb|A self-flowing hot spring “{{ill|Higashi Onsen (Iōjima)|ja|東温泉|12=draft}}” facing the East China Sea ({{ill|Satsuma Iōjima|ja|硫黄島 (鹿児島県)|12=draft}})]] '''Rotenburo''' (open-air bath) is a type of {{ill|Bath|ja|風呂|12=draft}} built outdoors with no roof or enclosure[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82-663841 Kotobank - Digital Daijisen (Sanseidō)]. It is also called '''Noburo''' (野風呂) or '''Notenburo''' (野天風呂)When referred to as 野天風呂 (or 野風呂), it may mean a bath that is not entirely in its natural state like a {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|12=draft}} but lacks as much development in walls or roofing as a typical open-air bath.. == Overview == Open-air baths can be considered the original “landscape” of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|12=draft}} bathing. They began as naturally emerging hot springs like {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|12=draft}}, in which a simple bathing area (bathtub) was arranged. Rotenburo (open-air baths) are highly popular at hot spring resorts. Reasons include being able to enjoy scenic views while bathing, experiencing a sense of openness, and having less risk of overheating (since heat does not accumulate as much as in an indoor setting). As a result, many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|旅館|12=draft}} and {{ill|Day-use hot spring facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|12=draft}} offer open-air baths in addition to {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|12=draft}}. In recent years, not only traditional onsen but also many {{ill|Supersento|ja|スーパー銭湯|1=Super Sento|12=draft}} have installed open-air baths. Up through the early 1980s, indoor styles such as {{ill|Roman bath|ja|ローマ風呂|12=draft}} or {{ill|Jungle bath|ja|ジャングル風呂|12=draft}} were mainstream. However, around the late 1980s during the {{ill|Japanese asset price bubble|ja|バブル景気|12=draft}}, rotenburo began to draw more attention. Sometimes, even a bath that has a ceiling and three walls (thus feeling nearly as enclosed as an indoor bath) might be marketed as a “rotenburo,” or described as “semi-open-air” (“半露天”). == Rotenburo Banzuke == '''Rotenburo Banzuke''' (露天風呂番付) is a form of {{ill|Onsen banzuke|ja|温泉番付|12=draft}}, ranking hot spring areas that are famous for their open-air baths. It was compiled in 1981, edited by '''Noguchi Fuyuto'''. In this ranking, the Western {{ill|Yokozuna|ja|横綱|12=draft}} is the “Sunayu” at {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|12=draft}}, and the Eastern Yokozuna is {{ill|Takaragawa Onsen|ja|宝川温泉|12=draft}}. At Yubara Onsen, there is a large monument depicting this Rotenburo Banzuke, serving as a symbol of the hot spring resort. == Variations of Open-Air Baths == Yumura onsen20n4592.jpg|Open-air bath in a forest setting
({{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|12=draft}}) Ryokukeitouen Kirishima City Kagoshima Pref09n4592.jpg|Open-air bath at a mountain stream
({{ill|Sakaenoo Onsen|ja|栄之尾温泉|12=draft}}) 190714 Settuskyo Onsen Takatsuki Osaka pref Japan01s3.jpg|Open-air bath with a gorge view
({{ill|Settsukyō Onsen|ja|摂津峡温泉|12=draft}}) Alpa Komakusa 2008.jpg|Open-air bath with a lake view (Tazawako Kōgen Onsen “Alpa Komakusa”) Tamatsukuri onsen yado01s3648.jpg|A guest room with an attached open-air bath
({{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉|12=draft}}) Koeda-Fudousan Katsura Koeda P6237036.JPG|A personal open-air bath built on private villa grounds == Management == [[File:新穂高の湯001.jpg|thumb|Cleaning an open-air bath
({{ill|Shinhodaka Onsen|ja|新穂高温泉|12=draft}})]] From a management perspective, especially with a free-flowing hot spring of high source temperature, weather changes can significantly alter the bath’s temperature, requiring more precise temperature control. Additionally, due to the constant possibility of contamination by {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ|12=draft}} bacteria, stricter hygiene measures than those for indoor baths are neededJapan Association of Living and Environmental Health “[http://www.seiei.or.jp/db-cho/le-11.html Legionella Prevention Guidelines]” (Revised March 1999; accessed January 5, 2016).. == Other == [[File:Iceland - Blue Lagoon 09 (6571266721).jpg|thumb|{{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft}} ({{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft}})]] * The open-air bath “Edo-jōwa Yoichi” at {{ill|Spa Resort Hawaiians|ja|スパリゾートハワイアンズ|12=draft}} in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}} is listed in the “{{ill|Guinness World Records|ja|ギネス世界記録|12=draft}} 2009” as the world’s largest open-air bath with a total tub area of 1000m²{{Cite web|和書|url=http://blog.hawaiians.co.jp/archives/50736632.html|title=世界一の大露天風呂|publisher=スパリゾートハワイアンズ|date=2008-11-08|accessdate=2021-06-03}}. * In {{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft}}, there is an open-air spa called the {{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft}} that covers about 5,000m² (with approximately 2,400m² open for general bathing)Iceland Tourism & Culture Research Institute “[http://www.iceland-kankobunka.jp/guide/reykjanes/blgn1.htm Tourist Spot Guide: Blue Lagoon]” (accessed January 5, 2016).. * {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|12=draft}} is “Open-Air Bath Day” (a pun on “Ro (6) Ten (.) Bu (2) Ro (6)”). It was established on June 26, 1987, by {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|12=draft}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}Conceived in 1987 as part of a local revitalization project by young people in Yubara Onsen.[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html Official site of the 6.26 Open-Air Bath Day Committee] (Yubara Town Ryokan Cooperative). == Notes == == Sources == == Related topics == * {{ill|part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburoでの組織的盗撮事件|12=draft|1=Organized voyeurism case in an open-air bath|3=露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件}} == External links == * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm Open-Air Bath Banzuke Chart] {{Onsen-stub}} '''Rotenburo''' (露天風呂) {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs]] [[Category:Bathing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:露天風呂]] [[ja:%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82]] [[ko:%EB%85%B8%EC%B2%9C%ED%83%95]] [[sn:Rotenburo]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rotenburo}}

({{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉|12=draft}}) Koeda-Fudousan Katsura Koeda P6237036.JPG|A personal open-air bath built on private villa grounds == Management == [[File:新穂高の湯001.jpg|thumb|Cleaning an open-air bath

Additionally, due to the constant possibility of contamination by {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ|12=draft}} bacteria, stricter hygiene measures than those for indoor baths are neededJapan Association of Living and Environmental Health “[http://www.seiei.or.jp/db-cho/le-11.html Legionella Prevention Guidelines]” (Revised March 1999; accessed January 5, 2016).. == Other == [[File:Iceland - Blue Lagoon 09 (6571266721).jpg|thumb|{{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft}} ({{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft}})]] * The open-air bath “Edo-jōwa Yoichi” at {{ill|Spa Resort Hawaiians|ja|スパリゾートハワイアンズ|12=draft}} in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}} is listed in the “{{ill|Guinness World Records|ja|ギネス世界記録|12=draft}} 2009” as the world’s largest open-air bath with a total tub area of 1000m²{{Cite web|和書|url=http://blog.hawaiians.co.jp/archives/50736632.html|title=世界一の大露天風呂|publisher=スパリゾートハワイアンズ|date=2008-11-08|accessdate=2021-06-03}}. * In {{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft}}, there is an open-air spa called the {{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft}} that covers about 5,000m² (with approximately 2,400m² open for general bathing)Iceland Tourism & Culture Research Institute “[http://www.iceland-kankobunka.jp/guide/reykjanes/blgn1.htm Tourist Spot Guide: Blue Lagoon]” (accessed January 5, 2016).. * {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|12=draft}} is “Open-Air Bath Day” (a pun on “Ro (6) Ten (.) Bu (2) Ro (6)”). It was established on June 26, 1987, by {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|12=draft}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}Conceived in 1987 as part of a local revitalization project by young people in Yubara Onsen.[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html Official site of the 6.26 Open-Air Bath Day Committee] (Yubara Town Ryokan Cooperative). == Notes == == Sources == == Related topics == * {{ill|part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburoでの組織的盗撮事件|12=draft|1=Organized voyeurism case in an open-air bath|3=露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件|1=Organized Voyeurism Scandal at Rotenburo baths}} == External links == * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm Open-Air Bath Banzuke Chart] {{Onsen-stub}} '''Rotenburo''' (露天風呂) {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs]] [[Category:Bathing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:露天風呂]] [[ja:%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82]] [[ko:%EB%85%B8%EC%B2%9C%ED%83%95]] [[sn:Rotenburo]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rotenburo}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|日帰り入浴施設|" to "|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1={{ill|Sotoyu|ja|外湯|12=draft}} refers to communal baths outside lodging facilities shared by multiple users, {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|12=draft}} denotes in-house hot spring baths privately installed within lodging facilities, and {{ill|Rotenburo|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|12=draft}} describes open-air baths typically without a roof or full enclosure.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:41, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Onsen in Nachikatsuura, Japan.jpg|thumb|280px|An open-air bath overlooking the sea ({{ill|Nanki-Katsuura Onsen|ja|南紀勝浦温泉|12=draft}})]] [[File:Iwojima Higashi onsen.jpg|280px|thumb|A self-flowing hot spring “{{ill|Higashi Onsen (Iōjima)|ja|東温泉|12=draft}}” facing the East China Sea ({{ill|Satsuma Iōjima|ja|硫黄島 (鹿児島県)|12=draft}})]] '''Rotenburo''' (open-air bath) is a type of {{ill|Bath|ja|風呂|12=draft}} built outdoors with no roof or enclosure[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82-663841 Kotobank - Digital Daijisen (Sanseidō)]. It is also called '''Noburo''' (野風呂) or '''Notenburo''' (野天風呂)When referred to as 野天風呂 (or 野風呂), it may mean a bath that is not entirely in its natural state like a {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|12=draft}} but lacks as much development in walls or roofing as a typical open-air bath.. == Overview == Open-air baths can be considered the original “landscape” of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|12=draft}} bathing. They began as naturally emerging hot springs like {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|12=draft}}, in which a simple bathing area (bathtub) was arranged. Rotenburo (open-air baths) are highly popular at hot spring resorts. Reasons include being able to enjoy scenic views while bathing, experiencing a sense of openness, and having less risk of overheating (since heat does not accumulate as much as in an indoor setting). As a result, many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|旅館|12=draft}} and {{ill|Day-use hot spring facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|12=draft}} offer open-air baths in addition to {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|12=draft}}. In recent years, not only traditional onsen but also many {{ill|Supersento|ja|スーパー銭湯|1=Super Sento|12=draft}} have installed open-air baths. Up through the early 1980s, indoor styles such as {{ill|Roman bath|ja|ローマ風呂|12=draft}} or {{ill|Jungle bath|ja|ジャングル風呂|12=draft}} were mainstream. However, around the late 1980s during the {{ill|Japanese asset price bubble|ja|バブル景気|12=draft}}, rotenburo began to draw more attention. Sometimes, even a bath that has a ceiling and three walls (thus feeling nearly as enclosed as an indoor bath) might be marketed as a “rotenburo,” or described as “semi-open-air” (“半露天”). == Rotenburo Banzuke == '''Rotenburo Banzuke''' (露天風呂番付) is a form of {{ill|Onsen banzuke|ja|温泉番付|12=draft}}, ranking hot spring areas that are famous for their open-air baths. It was compiled in 1981, edited by '''Noguchi Fuyuto'''. In this ranking, the Western {{ill|Yokozuna|ja|横綱|12=draft}} is the “Sunayu” at {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|12=draft}}, and the Eastern Yokozuna is {{ill|Takaragawa Onsen|ja|宝川温泉|12=draft}}. At Yubara Onsen, there is a large monument depicting this Rotenburo Banzuke, serving as a symbol of the hot spring resort. == Variations of Open-Air Baths == Yumura onsen20n4592.jpg|Open-air bath in a forest setting
({{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|12=draft}}) Ryokukeitouen Kirishima City Kagoshima Pref09n4592.jpg|Open-air bath at a mountain stream
({{ill|Sakaenoo Onsen|ja|栄之尾温泉|12=draft}}) 190714 Settuskyo Onsen Takatsuki Osaka pref Japan01s3.jpg|Open-air bath with a gorge view
({{ill|Settsukyō Onsen|ja|摂津峡温泉|12=draft}}) Alpa Komakusa 2008.jpg|Open-air bath with a lake view (Tazawako Kōgen Onsen “Alpa Komakusa”) Tamatsukuri onsen yado01s3648.jpg|A guest room with an attached open-air bath
({{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉|12=draft}}) Koeda-Fudousan Katsura Koeda P6237036.JPG|A personal open-air bath built on private villa grounds == Management == [[File:新穂高の湯001.jpg|thumb|Cleaning an open-air bath
({{ill|Shinhodaka Onsen|ja|新穂高温泉|12=draft}})]] From a management perspective, especially with a free-flowing hot spring of high source temperature, weather changes can significantly alter the bath’s temperature, requiring more precise temperature control. Additionally, due to the constant possibility of contamination by {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ|12=draft}} bacteria, stricter hygiene measures than those for indoor baths are neededJapan Association of Living and Environmental Health “[http://www.seiei.or.jp/db-cho/le-11.html Legionella Prevention Guidelines]” (Revised March 1999; accessed January 5, 2016).. == Other == [[File:Iceland - Blue Lagoon 09 (6571266721).jpg|thumb|{{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft}} ({{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft}})]] * The open-air bath “Edo-jōwa Yoichi” at {{ill|Spa Resort Hawaiians|ja|スパリゾートハワイアンズ|12=draft}} in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}} is listed in the “{{ill|Guinness World Records|ja|ギネス世界記録|12=draft}} 2009” as the world’s largest open-air bath with a total tub area of 1000m²{{Cite web|和書|url=http://blog.hawaiians.co.jp/archives/50736632.html|title=世界一の大露天風呂|publisher=スパリゾートハワイアンズ|date=2008-11-08|accessdate=2021-06-03}}. * In {{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft}}, there is an open-air spa called the {{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft}} that covers about 5,000m² (with approximately 2,400m² open for general bathing)Iceland Tourism & Culture Research Institute “[http://www.iceland-kankobunka.jp/guide/reykjanes/blgn1.htm Tourist Spot Guide: Blue Lagoon]” (accessed January 5, 2016).. * {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|12=draft}} is “Open-Air Bath Day” (a pun on “Ro (6) Ten (.) Bu (2) Ro (6)”). It was established on June 26, 1987, by {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|12=draft}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}Conceived in 1987 as part of a local revitalization project by young people in Yubara Onsen.[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html Official site of the 6.26 Open-Air Bath Day Committee] (Yubara Town Ryokan Cooperative). == Notes == == Sources == == Related topics == * {{ill|part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburoでの組織的盗撮事件|12=draft|1=Organized voyeurism case in an open-air bath|3=露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件|1=Organized Voyeurism Scandal at Rotenburo baths}} == External links == * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm Open-Air Bath Banzuke Chart] {{Onsen-stub}} '''Rotenburo''' (露天風呂) {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs]] [[Category:Bathing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:露天風呂]] [[ja:%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82]] [[ko:%EB%85%B8%EC%B2%9C%ED%83%95]] [[sn:Rotenburo]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rotenburo}}

Additionally, due to the constant possibility of contamination by {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ|12=draft}} bacteria, stricter hygiene measures than those for indoor baths are neededJapan Association of Living and Environmental Health “[http://www.seiei.or.jp/db-cho/le-11.html Legionella Prevention Guidelines]” (Revised March 1999; accessed January 5, 2016).. == Other == [[File:Iceland - Blue Lagoon 09 (6571266721).jpg|thumb|{{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft}} ({{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft}})]] * The open-air bath “Edo-jōwa Yoichi” at {{ill|Spa Resort Hawaiians|ja|スパリゾートハワイアンズ|12=draft}} in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}} is listed in the “{{ill|Guinness World Records|ja|ギネス世界記録|12=draft}} 2009” as the world’s largest open-air bath with a total tub area of 1000m²{{Cite web|和書|url=http://blog.hawaiians.co.jp/archives/50736632.html|title=世界一の大露天風呂|publisher=スパリゾートハワイアンズ|date=2008-11-08|accessdate=2021-06-03}}. * In {{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft}}, there is an open-air spa called the {{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon}} that covers about 5,000m² (with approximately 2,400m² open for general bathing)Iceland Tourism & Culture Research Institute “[http://www.iceland-kankobunka.jp/guide/reykjanes/blgn1.htm Tourist Spot Guide: Blue Lagoon]” (accessed January 5, 2016).. * {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|12=draft}} is “Open-Air Bath Day” (a pun on “Ro (6) Ten (.) Bu (2) Ro (6)”). It was established on June 26, 1987, by {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|12=draft}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}Conceived in 1987 as part of a local revitalization project by young people in Yubara Onsen.[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html Official site of the 6.26 Open-Air Bath Day Committee] (Yubara Town Ryokan Cooperative). == Notes == == Sources == == Related topics == * {{ill|part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburoでの組織的盗撮事件|12=draft|1=Organized voyeurism case in an open-air bath|3=露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件|1=Organized Voyeurism Scandal at Rotenburo baths}} == External links == * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm Open-Air Bath Banzuke Chart] {{Onsen-stub}} '''Rotenburo''' (露天風呂) {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs]] [[Category:Bathing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:露天風呂]] [[ja:%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82]] [[ko:%EB%85%B8%EC%B2%9C%ED%83%95]] [[sn:Rotenburo]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rotenburo}}

{{afc comment|1={{ill|Sotoyu|ja|外湯|12=draft}} refers to communal baths outside lodging facilities shared by multiple users, {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|12=draft}} denotes in-house hot spring baths privately installed within lodging facilities, and {{ill|Rotenburo|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburo|12=draft}} describes open-air baths typically without a roof or full enclosure.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:41, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Onsen in Nachikatsuura, Japan.jpg|thumb|280px|An open-air bath overlooking the sea ({{ill|Nanki-Katsuura Onsen|ja|南紀勝浦温泉|12=draft}})]] [[File:Iwojima Higashi onsen.jpg|280px|thumb|A self-flowing hot spring “{{ill|Higashi Onsen (Iōjima)|ja|東温泉|12=draft}}” facing the East China Sea ({{ill|Satsuma Iōjima|ja|硫黄島 (鹿児島県)|12=draft}})]] '''Rotenburo''' (open-air bath) is a type of {{ill|Bath|ja|風呂|12=draft}} built outdoors with no roof or enclosure[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82-663841 Kotobank - Digital Daijisen (Sanseidō)]. It is also called '''Noburo''' (野風呂) or '''Notenburo''' (野天風呂)When referred to as 野天風呂 (or 野風呂), it may mean a bath that is not entirely in its natural state like a {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|12=draft}} but lacks as much development in walls or roofing as a typical open-air bath.. == Overview == Open-air baths can be considered the original “landscape” of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|12=draft}} bathing. They began as naturally emerging hot springs like {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|12=draft}}, in which a simple bathing area (bathtub) was arranged. Rotenburo (open-air baths) are highly popular at hot spring resorts. Reasons include being able to enjoy scenic views while bathing, experiencing a sense of openness, and having less risk of overheating (since heat does not accumulate as much as in an indoor setting). As a result, many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|旅館|12=draft}} and {{ill|Day-use hot spring facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|12=draft}} offer open-air baths in addition to {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|12=draft}}. In recent years, not only traditional onsen but also many {{ill|Supersento|ja|スーパー銭湯|1=Super Sento|12=draft}} have installed open-air baths. Up through the early 1980s, indoor styles such as {{ill|Roman bath|ja|ローマ風呂|12=draft}} or {{ill|Jungle bath|ja|ジャングル風呂|12=draft}} were mainstream. However, around the late 1980s during the {{ill|Japanese asset price bubble|ja|バブル景気|12=draft}}, rotenburo began to draw more attention. Sometimes, even a bath that has a ceiling and three walls (thus feeling nearly as enclosed as an indoor bath) might be marketed as a “rotenburo,” or described as “semi-open-air” (“半露天”). == Rotenburo Banzuke == '''Rotenburo Banzuke''' (露天風呂番付) is a form of {{ill|Onsen banzuke|ja|温泉番付|12=draft}}, ranking hot spring areas that are famous for their open-air baths. It was compiled in 1981, edited by '''Noguchi Fuyuto'''. In this ranking, the Western {{ill|Yokozuna|ja|横綱|12=draft}} is the “Sunayu” at {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|12=draft}}, and the Eastern Yokozuna is {{ill|Takaragawa Onsen|ja|宝川温泉|12=draft}}. At Yubara Onsen, there is a large monument depicting this Rotenburo Banzuke, serving as a symbol of the hot spring resort. == Variations of Open-Air Baths == Yumura onsen20n4592.jpg|Open-air bath in a forest setting
({{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|12=draft}}) Ryokukeitouen Kirishima City Kagoshima Pref09n4592.jpg|Open-air bath at a mountain stream
({{ill|Sakaenoo Onsen|ja|栄之尾温泉|12=draft}}) 190714 Settuskyo Onsen Takatsuki Osaka pref Japan01s3.jpg|Open-air bath with a gorge view
({{ill|Settsukyō Onsen|ja|摂津峡温泉|12=draft}}) Alpa Komakusa 2008.jpg|Open-air bath with a lake view (Tazawako Kōgen Onsen “Alpa Komakusa”) Tamatsukuri onsen yado01s3648.jpg|A guest room with an attached open-air bath
({{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉|12=draft}}) Koeda-Fudousan Katsura Koeda P6237036.JPG|A personal open-air bath built on private villa grounds == Management == [[File:新穂高の湯001.jpg|thumb|Cleaning an open-air bath
({{ill|Shinhodaka Onsen|ja|新穂高温泉|12=draft}})]] From a management perspective, especially with a free-flowing hot spring of high source temperature, weather changes can significantly alter the bath’s temperature, requiring more precise temperature control. Additionally, due to the constant possibility of contamination by {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ|12=draft}} bacteria, stricter hygiene measures than those for indoor baths are neededJapan Association of Living and Environmental Health “[http://www.seiei.or.jp/db-cho/le-11.html Legionella Prevention Guidelines]” (Revised March 1999; accessed January 5, 2016).. == Other == [[File:Iceland - Blue Lagoon 09 (6571266721).jpg|thumb|{{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon}} ({{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft}})]] * The open-air bath “Edo-jōwa Yoichi” at {{ill|Spa Resort Hawaiians|ja|スパリゾートハワイアンズ|12=draft}} in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}} is listed in the “{{ill|Guinness World Records|ja|ギネス世界記録|12=draft}} 2009” as the world’s largest open-air bath with a total tub area of 1000m²{{Cite web|和書|url=http://blog.hawaiians.co.jp/archives/50736632.html|title=世界一の大露天風呂|publisher=スパリゾートハワイアンズ|date=2008-11-08|accessdate=2021-06-03}}. * In {{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft}}, there is an open-air spa called the {{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon}} that covers about 5,000m² (with approximately 2,400m² open for general bathing)Iceland Tourism & Culture Research Institute “[http://www.iceland-kankobunka.jp/guide/reykjanes/blgn1.htm Tourist Spot Guide: Blue Lagoon]” (accessed January 5, 2016).. * {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|12=draft}} is “Open-Air Bath Day” (a pun on “Ro (6) Ten (.) Bu (2) Ro (6)”). It was established on June 26, 1987, by {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|12=draft}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}Conceived in 1987 as part of a local revitalization project by young people in Yubara Onsen.[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html Official site of the 6.26 Open-Air Bath Day Committee] (Yubara Town Ryokan Cooperative). == Notes == == Sources == == Related topics == * {{ill|part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburoでの組織的盗撮事件|12=draft|1=Organized voyeurism case in an open-air bath|3=露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件|1=Organized Voyeurism Scandal at Rotenburo baths}} == External links == * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm Open-Air Bath Banzuke Chart] {{Onsen-stub}} '''Rotenburo''' (露天風呂) {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs]] [[Category:Bathing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:露天風呂]] [[ja:%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82]] [[ko:%EB%85%B8%EC%B2%9C%ED%83%95]] [[sn:Rotenburo]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rotenburo}}

({{ill|Settsukyō Onsen|ja|摂津峡温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}) Alpa Komakusa 2008.jpg|Open-air bath with a lake view (Tazawako Kōgen Onsen “Alpa Komakusa”) Tamatsukuri onsen yado01s3648.jpg|A guest room with an attached open-air bath
({{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}) Koeda-Fudousan Katsura Koeda P6237036.JPG|A personal open-air bath built on private villa grounds == Management == [[File:新穂高の湯001.jpg|thumb|Cleaning an open-air bath
({{ill|Shinhodaka Onsen|ja|新穂高温泉|12=draft|12=draft}})]] From a management perspective, especially with a free-flowing hot spring of high source temperature, weather changes can significantly alter the bath’s temperature, requiring more precise temperature control. Additionally, due to the constant possibility of contamination by {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ|12=draft|12=draft}} bacteria, stricter hygiene measures than those for indoor baths are neededJapan Association of Living and Environmental Health “[http://www.seiei.or.jp/db-cho/le-11.html Legionella Prevention Guidelines]” (Revised March 1999; accessed January 5, 2016).. == Other == [[File:Iceland - Blue Lagoon 09 (6571266721).jpg|thumb|{{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon|12=draft}} ({{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft|12=draft}})]] * The open-air bath “Edo-jōwa Yoichi” at {{ill|Spa Resort Hawaiians|ja|スパリゾートハワイアンズ|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft|12=draft}} is listed in the “{{ill|Guinness World Records|ja|ギネス世界記録|12=draft|12=draft}} 2009” as the world’s largest open-air bath with a total tub area of 1000m²{{Cite web|和書|url=http://blog.hawaiians.co.jp/archives/50736632.html|title=世界一の大露天風呂|publisher=スパリゾートハワイアンズ|date=2008-11-08|accessdate=2021-06-03}}. * In {{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft|12=draft}}, there is an open-air spa called the {{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon|12=draft}} that covers about 5,000m² (with approximately 2,400m² open for general bathing)Iceland Tourism & Culture Research Institute “[http://www.iceland-kankobunka.jp/guide/reykjanes/blgn1.htm Tourist Spot Guide: Blue Lagoon]” (accessed January 5, 2016).. * {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|12=draft|12=draft}} is “Open-Air Bath Day” (a pun on “Ro (6) Ten (.) Bu (2) Ro (6)”). It was established on June 26, 1987, by {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft|12=draft}}Conceived in 1987 as part of a local revitalization project by young people in Yubara Onsen.[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html Official site of the 6.26 Open-Air Bath Day Committee] (Yubara Town Ryokan Cooperative). == Notes == == Sources == == Related topics == * {{ill|part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburoでの組織的盗撮事件|12=draft|1=Organized voyeurism case in an open-air bath|3=露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件|1=Organized Voyeurism Scandal at Rotenburo baths|12=draft}} == External links == * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm Open-Air Bath Banzuke Chart] {{Onsen-stub}} '''Rotenburo''' (露天風呂) {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs]] [[Category:Bathing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Rotenburo]] [[ja:露天風呂]] [[ja:%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82]] [[ko:%EB%85%B8%EC%B2%9C%ED%83%95]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rotenburo}}

{{afc comment|1={{ill|Sotoyu|ja|外湯|12=draft|12=draft}} refers to communal baths outside lodging facilities shared by multiple users, {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|12=draft|12=draft}} denotes in-house hot spring baths privately installed within lodging facilities, and {{ill|Rotenburo|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburo|12=draft|12=draft}} describes open-air baths typically without a roof or full enclosure.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:41, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Onsen in Nachikatsuura, Japan.jpg|thumb|280px|An open-air bath overlooking the sea ({{ill|Nanki-Katsuura Onsen|ja|南紀勝浦温泉|12=draft|12=draft}})]] [[File:Iwojima Higashi onsen.jpg|280px|thumb|A self-flowing hot spring “{{ill|Higashi Onsen (Iōjima)|ja|東温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}” facing the East China Sea ({{ill|Satsuma Iōjima|ja|硫黄島 (鹿児島県)|12=draft|12=draft}})]] '''Rotenburo''' (open-air bath) is a type of {{ill|Bath|ja|風呂|12=draft|12=draft}} built outdoors with no roof or enclosure[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82-663841 Kotobank - Digital Daijisen (Sanseidō)]. It is also called '''Noburo''' (野風呂) or '''Notenburo''' (野天風呂)When referred to as 野天風呂 (or 野風呂), it may mean a bath that is not entirely in its natural state like a {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|12=draft|12=draft}} but lacks as much development in walls or roofing as a typical open-air bath.. == Overview == Open-air baths can be considered the original “landscape” of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|12=draft|12=draft}} bathing. They began as naturally emerging hot springs like {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|12=draft|12=draft}}, in which a simple bathing area (bathtub) was arranged. Rotenburo (open-air baths) are highly popular at hot spring resorts. Reasons include being able to enjoy scenic views while bathing, experiencing a sense of openness, and having less risk of overheating (since heat does not accumulate as much as in an indoor setting). As a result, many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|旅館|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Day-use hot spring facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|12=draft|12=draft}} offer open-air baths in addition to {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|12=draft|12=draft}}. In recent years, not only traditional onsen but also many {{ill|Supersento|ja|スーパー銭湯|1=Super Sento|12=draft|12=draft}} have installed open-air baths. Up through the early 1980s, indoor styles such as {{ill|Roman bath|ja|ローマ風呂|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Jungle bath|ja|ジャングル風呂|12=draft|12=draft}} were mainstream. However, around the late 1980s during the {{ill|Japanese asset price bubble|ja|バブル景気|12=draft|12=draft}}, rotenburo began to draw more attention. Sometimes, even a bath that has a ceiling and three walls (thus feeling nearly as enclosed as an indoor bath) might be marketed as a “rotenburo,” or described as “semi-open-air” (“半露天”). == Rotenburo Banzuke == '''Rotenburo Banzuke''' (露天風呂番付) is a form of {{ill|Onsen banzuke|ja|温泉番付|12=draft|12=draft}}, ranking hot spring areas that are famous for their open-air baths. It was compiled in 1981, edited by '''Noguchi Fuyuto'''. In this ranking, the Western {{ill|Yokozuna|ja|横綱|12=draft|12=draft}} is the “Sunayu” at {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the Eastern Yokozuna is {{ill|Takaragawa Onsen|ja|宝川温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}. At Yubara Onsen, there is a large monument depicting this Rotenburo Banzuke, serving as a symbol of the hot spring resort. == Variations of Open-Air Baths == Yumura onsen20n4592.jpg|Open-air bath in a forest setting
({{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|12=draft|lt=Yumura Onsen}}) Ryokukeitouen Kirishima City Kagoshima Pref09n4592.jpg|Open-air bath at a mountain stream
({{ill|Sakaenoo Onsen|ja|栄之尾温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}) 190714 Settuskyo Onsen Takatsuki Osaka pref Japan01s3.jpg|Open-air bath with a gorge view
({{ill|Settsukyō Onsen|ja|摂津峡温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}) Alpa Komakusa 2008.jpg|Open-air bath with a lake view (Tazawako Kōgen Onsen “Alpa Komakusa”) Tamatsukuri onsen yado01s3648.jpg|A guest room with an attached open-air bath
({{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}) Koeda-Fudousan Katsura Koeda P6237036.JPG|A personal open-air bath built on private villa grounds == Management == [[File:新穂高の湯001.jpg|thumb|Cleaning an open-air bath
({{ill|Shinhodaka Onsen|ja|新穂高温泉|12=draft|12=draft}})]] From a management perspective, especially with a free-flowing hot spring of high source temperature, weather changes can significantly alter the bath’s temperature, requiring more precise temperature control. Additionally, due to the constant possibility of contamination by {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ|12=draft|12=draft}} bacteria, stricter hygiene measures than those for indoor baths are neededJapan Association of Living and Environmental Health “[http://www.seiei.or.jp/db-cho/le-11.html Legionella Prevention Guidelines]” (Revised March 1999; accessed January 5, 2016).. == Other == [[File:Iceland - Blue Lagoon 09 (6571266721).jpg|thumb|{{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon|12=draft}} ({{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft|12=draft}})]] * The open-air bath “Edo-jōwa Yoichi” at {{ill|Spa Resort Hawaiians|ja|スパリゾートハワイアンズ|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft|12=draft}} is listed in the “{{ill|Guinness World Records|ja|ギネス世界記録|12=draft|12=draft}} 2009” as the world’s largest open-air bath with a total tub area of 1000m²{{Cite web|和書|url=http://blog.hawaiians.co.jp/archives/50736632.html|title=世界一の大露天風呂|publisher=スパリゾートハワイアンズ|date=2008-11-08|accessdate=2021-06-03}}. * In {{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft|12=draft}}, there is an open-air spa called the {{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon|12=draft}} that covers about 5,000m² (with approximately 2,400m² open for general bathing)Iceland Tourism & Culture Research Institute “[http://www.iceland-kankobunka.jp/guide/reykjanes/blgn1.htm Tourist Spot Guide: Blue Lagoon]” (accessed January 5, 2016).. * {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|12=draft|12=draft}} is “Open-Air Bath Day” (a pun on “Ro (6) Ten (.) Bu (2) Ro (6)”). It was established on June 26, 1987, by {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft|12=draft}}Conceived in 1987 as part of a local revitalization project by young people in Yubara Onsen.[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html Official site of the 6.26 Open-Air Bath Day Committee] (Yubara Town Ryokan Cooperative). == Notes == == Sources == == Related topics == * {{ill|part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburoでの組織的盗撮事件|12=draft|1=Organized voyeurism case in an open-air bath|3=露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件|1=Organized Voyeurism Scandal at Rotenburo baths|12=draft}} == External links == * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm Open-Air Bath Banzuke Chart] {{Onsen-stub}} '''Rotenburo''' (露天風呂) {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs]] [[Category:Bathing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Rotenburo]] [[ja:露天風呂]] [[ja:%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82]] [[ko:%EB%85%B8%EC%B2%9C%ED%83%95]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rotenburo}}

Sometimes, even a bath that has a ceiling and three walls (thus feeling nearly as enclosed as an indoor bath) might be marketed as a “rotenburo,” or described as “semi-open-air” (“半露天”). == Rotenburo Banzuke == '''Rotenburo Banzuke''' (露天風呂番付) is a form of {{ill|Onsen banzuke|ja|温泉番付|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ranking hot spring areas that are famous for their open-air baths. It was compiled in 1981, edited by '''Noguchi Fuyuto'''. In this Ranking, the Western {{ill|Yokozuna|ja|横綱|12=draft|12=draft}} is the “Sunayu” at {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the Eastern Yokozuna is {{ill|Takaragawa Onsen|ja|宝川温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}. At Yubara Onsen, there is a large monument depicting this Rotenburo Banzuke, serving as a symbol of the hot spring resort. == Variations of Open-Air Baths == Yumura onsen20n4592.jpg|Open-air bath in a forest setting
({{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|12=draft|lt=Yumura Onsen}}) Ryokukeitouen Kirishima City Kagoshima Pref09n4592.jpg|Open-air bath at a mountain stream
({{ill|Sakaenoo Onsen|ja|栄之尾温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}) 190714 Settuskyo Onsen Takatsuki Osaka pref Japan01s3.jpg|Open-air bath with a gorge view
({{ill|Settsukyō Onsen|ja|摂津峡温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}) Alpa Komakusa 2008.jpg|Open-air bath with a lake view (Tazawako Kōgen Onsen “Alpa Komakusa”) Tamatsukuri onsen yado01s3648.jpg|A guest room with an attached open-air bath
({{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}) Koeda-Fudousan Katsura Koeda P6237036.JPG|A personal open-air bath built on private villa grounds == Management == [[File:新穂高の湯001.jpg|thumb|Cleaning an open-air bath
({{ill|Shinhodaka Onsen|ja|新穂高温泉|12=draft|12=draft}})]] From a management perspective, especially with a free-flowing hot spring of high source temperature, weather changes can significantly alter the bath’s temperature, requiring more precise temperature control. Additionally, due to the constant possibility of contamination by {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ|12=draft|12=draft}} bacteria, stricter hygiene measures than those for indoor baths are neededJapan Association of Living and Environmental Health “[http://www.seiei.or.jp/db-cho/le-11.html Legionella Prevention Guidelines]” (Revised March 1999; accessed January 5, 2016).. == Other == [[File:Iceland - Blue Lagoon 09 (6571266721).jpg|thumb|{{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon|12=draft}} ({{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft|12=draft}})]] * The open-air bath “Edo-jōwa Yoichi” at {{ill|Spa Resort Hawaiians|ja|スパリゾートハワイアンズ|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft|12=draft}} is listed in the “{{ill|Guinness World Records|ja|ギネス世界記録|12=draft|12=draft}} 2009” as the world’s largest open-air bath with a total tub area of 1000m²{{Cite web|和書|url=http://blog.hawaiians.co.jp/archives/50736632.html|title=世界一の大露天風呂|publisher=スパリゾートハワイアンズ|date=2008-11-08|accessdate=2021-06-03}}. * In {{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft|12=draft}}, there is an open-air spa called the {{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon|12=draft}} that covers about 5,000m² (with approximately 2,400m² open for general bathing)Iceland Tourism & Culture Research Institute “[http://www.iceland-kankobunka.jp/guide/reykjanes/blgn1.htm Tourist Spot Guide: Blue Lagoon]” (accessed January 5, 2016).. * {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|12=draft|12=draft}} is “Open-Air Bath Day” (a pun on “Ro (6) Ten (.) Bu (2) Ro (6)”). It was established on June 26, 1987, by {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft|12=draft}}Conceived in 1987 as part of a local revitalization project by young people in Yubara Onsen.[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html Official site of the 6.26 Open-Air Bath Day Committee] (Yubara Town Ryokan Cooperative). == Notes == == Sources == == Related topics == * {{ill|part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburoでの組織的盗撮事件|12=draft|1=Organized voyeurism case in an open-air bath|3=露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件|1=Organized Voyeurism Scandal at Rotenburo baths|12=draft}} == External links == * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm Open-Air Bath Banzuke Chart] {{Onsen-stub}} '''Rotenburo''' (露天風呂) {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs]] [[Category:Bathing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Rotenburo]] [[ja:露天風呂]] [[ja:%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82]] [[ko:%EB%85%B8%EC%B2%9C%ED%83%95]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rotenburo}}

{{afc comment|1={{ill|Sotoyu|ja|外湯|12=draft|12=draft}} refers to communal baths outside lodging facilities shared by multiple users, {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|12=draft|12=draft}} denotes in-house hot spring baths privately installed within lodging facilities, and {{ill|Rotenburo|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburo|12=draft|12=draft}} describes open-air baths typically without a roof or full enclosure.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:41, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Onsen in Nachikatsuura, Japan.jpg|thumb|280px|An open-air bath overlooking the sea ({{ill|Nanki-Katsuura Onsen|ja|南紀勝浦温泉|12=draft|12=draft}})]] [[File:Iwojima Higashi onsen.jpg|280px|thumb|A self-flowing hot spring “{{ill|Higashi Onsen (Iōjima)|ja|東温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}” facing the East China Sea ({{ill|Satsuma Iōjima|ja|硫黄島 (鹿児島県)|12=draft|12=draft}})]] '''Rotenburo''' (open-air bath) is a type of {{ill|Bath|ja|風呂|12=draft|12=draft}} built outdoors with no roof or enclosure[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82-663841 Kotobank - Digital Daijisen (Sanseidō)]. It is also called '''Noburo''' (野風呂) or '''Notenburo''' (野天風呂)When referred to as 野天風呂 (or 野風呂), it may mean a bath that is not entirely in its natural state like a {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|12=draft|12=draft}} but lacks as much development in walls or roofing as a typical open-air bath.. == Overview == Open-air baths can be considered the original “landscape” of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|12=draft|12=draft}} bathing. They began as naturally emerging hot springs like {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|12=draft|12=draft}}, in which a simple bathing area (bathtub) was arranged. Rotenburo (open-air baths) are highly popular at hot spring resorts. Reasons include being able to enjoy scenic views while bathing, experiencing a sense of openness, and having less risk of overheating (since heat does not accumulate as much as in an indoor setting). As a result, many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|旅館|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Day-use hot spring facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|12=draft|12=draft}} offer open-air baths in addition to {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|12=draft|12=draft}}. In recent years, not only traditional onsen but also many {{ill|Supersento|ja|スーパー銭湯|1=Super Sento|12=draft|12=draft}} have installed open-air baths. Up through the early 1980s, indoor styles such as {{ill|Roman bath|ja|ローマ風呂|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Jungle bath|ja|ジャングル風呂|12=draft|12=draft}} were mainstream. However, around the late 1980s during the {{ill|Japanese asset price bubble|ja|バブル景気|12=draft|12=draft}}, rotenburo began to draw more attention. Sometimes, even a bath that has a ceiling and three walls (thus feeling nearly as enclosed as an indoor bath) might be marketed as a “rotenburo,” or described as “semi-open-air” (“半露天”). == Rotenburo Banzuke == '''Rotenburo Banzuke''' (露天風呂番付) is a form of {{ill|Onsen banzuke|ja|温泉番付|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ranking hot spring areas that are famous for their open-air baths. It was compiled in 1981, edited by '''Noguchi Fuyuto'''. In this Ranking, the Western {{ill|Yokozuna|ja|横綱|12=draft|12=draft}} is the “Sunayu” at {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the Eastern Yokozuna is {{ill|Takaragawa Onsen|ja|宝川温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}. At Yubara Onsen, there is a large monument depicting this Rotenburo Banzuke, serving as a symbol of the hot spring resort. == Variations of Open-Air Baths == Yumura onsen20n4592.jpg|Open-air bath in a forest setting
({{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|12=draft|lt=Yumura Onsen}}) Ryokukeitouen Kirishima City Kagoshima Pref09n4592.jpg|Open-air bath at a mountain stream
({{ill|Sakaenoo Onsen|ja|栄之尾温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}) 190714 Settuskyo Onsen Takatsuki Osaka pref Japan01s3.jpg|Open-air bath with a gorge view
({{ill|Settsukyō Onsen|ja|摂津峡温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}) Alpa Komakusa 2008.jpg|Open-air bath with a lake view (Tazawako Kōgen Onsen “Alpa Komakusa”) Tamatsukuri onsen yado01s3648.jpg|A guest room with an attached open-air bath
({{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}) Koeda-Fudousan Katsura Koeda P6237036.JPG|A personal open-air bath built on private villa grounds == Management == [[File:新穂高の湯001.jpg|thumb|Cleaning an open-air bath
({{ill|Shinhodaka Onsen|ja|新穂高温泉|12=draft|12=draft}})]] From a management perspective, especially with a free-flowing hot spring of high source temperature, weather changes can significantly alter the bath’s temperature, requiring more precise temperature control. Additionally, due to the constant possibility of contamination by {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ|12=draft|12=draft}} bacteria, stricter hygiene measures than those for indoor baths are neededJapan Association of Living and Environmental Health “[http://www.seiei.or.jp/db-cho/le-11.html Legionella Prevention Guidelines]” (Revised March 1999; accessed January 5, 2016).. == Other == [[File:Iceland - Blue Lagoon 09 (6571266721).jpg|thumb|{{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon|12=draft}} ({{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft|12=draft}})]] * The open-air bath “Edo-jōwa Yoichi” at {{ill|Spa Resort Hawaiians|ja|スパリゾートハワイアンズ|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft|12=draft}} is listed in the “{{ill|Guinness World Records|ja|ギネス世界記録|12=draft|12=draft}} 2009” as the world’s largest open-air bath with a total tub area of 1000m²{{Cite web|和書|url=http://blog.hawaiians.co.jp/archives/50736632.html|title=世界一の大露天風呂|publisher=スパリゾートハワイアンズ|date=2008-11-08|accessdate=2021-06-03}}. * In {{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|12=draft|12=draft}}, there is an open-air spa called the {{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|12=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon|12=draft}} that covers about 5,000m² (with approximately 2,400m² open for general bathing)Iceland Tourism & Culture Research Institute “[http://www.iceland-kankobunka.jp/guide/reykjanes/blgn1.htm Tourist Spot Guide: Blue Lagoon]” (accessed January 5, 2016).. * {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|12=draft|12=draft}} is “Open-Air Bath Day” (a pun on “Ro (6) Ten (.) Bu (2) Ro (6)”). It was established on June 26, 1987, by {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft|12=draft}}Conceived in 1987 as part of a local revitalization project by young people in Yubara Onsen.[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html Official site of the 6.26 Open-Air Bath Day Committee] (Yubara Town Ryokan Cooperative). == Notes == == Sources == == Related topics == * {{ill|part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburoでの組織的盗撮事件|12=draft|1=Organized voyeurism case in an open-air bath|3=露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件|1=Organized Voyeurism Scandal at Rotenburo baths|12=draft}} == External links == * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm Open-Air Bath Banzuke Chart] {{Onsen-stub}} '''Rotenburo''' (露天風呂) {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs]] [[Category:Bathing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Rotenburo]] [[ja:露天風呂]] [[ja:%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82]] [[ko:%EB%85%B8%EC%B2%9C%ED%83%95]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rotenburo}}
