Bu (2) Ro (6)”). It was established on June 26, 1987, by {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft|12=draft}}Conceived in 1987 as part of a local revitalization project by young people in Yubara Onsen.[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html Official site of the 6.26 Open-Air Bath Day Committee] (Yubara Town Ryokan Cooperative). == Notes == == Sources == == Related topics == * {{ill|part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburoでの組織的盗撮事件|12=draft|1=Organized voyeurism case in an open-air bath|3=露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件|1=Organized Voyeurism Scandal at Rotenburo baths|12=draft}} == External links == * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm Open-Air Bath Banzuke Chart] {{Onsen-stub}} '''Rotenburo''' (露天風呂) {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs]] [[Category:Bathing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Rotenburo]] [[ja:露天風呂]] [[ja:%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82]] [[ko:%EB%85%B8%EC%B2%9C%ED%83%95]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rotenburo}}

{{afc comment|1={{ill|Sotoyu|ja|外湯|qq=draft|qq=draft}} refers to communal baths outside lodging facilities shared by multiple users, {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|qq=draft|qq=draft}} denotes in-house hot spring baths privately installed within lodging facilities, and {{ill|Rotenburo|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburo|qq=draft|qq=draft}} describes open-air baths typically without a roof or full enclosure.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:41, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Onsen in Nachikatsuura, Japan.jpg|thumb|280px|An open-air bath overlooking the sea ({{ill|Nanki-Katsuura Onsen|ja|南紀勝浦温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] [[File:Iwojima Higashi onsen.jpg|280px|thumb|A self-flowing hot spring “{{ill|Higashi Onsen (Iōjima)|ja|東温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}” facing the East China Sea ({{ill|Satsuma Iōjima|ja|硫黄島 (鹿児島県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] '''Rotenburo''' (open-air bath) is a type of {{ill|Bath|ja|風呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} built outdoors with no roof or enclosure[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82-663841 Kotobank - Digital Daijisen (Sanseidō)]. It is also called '''Noburo''' (野風呂) or '''Notenburo''' (野天風呂)When referred to as 野天風呂 (or 野風呂), it may mean a bath that is not entirely in its natural state like a {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|qq=draft|qq=draft}} but lacks as much development in walls or roofing as a typical open-air bath.. == Overview == Open-air baths can be considered the original “landscape” of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bathing. They began as naturally emerging hot springs like {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in which a simple bathing area (bathtub) was arranged. Rotenburo (open-air baths) are highly popular at hot spring resorts. Reasons include being able to enjoy scenic views while bathing, experiencing a sense of openness, and having less risk of overheating (since heat does not accumulate as much as in an indoor setting). As a result, many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|旅館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Day-use hot spring facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|qq=draft|qq=draft}} offer open-air baths in addition to {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In recent years, not only traditional onsen but also many {{ill|Supersento|ja|スーパー銭湯|1=Super Sento|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have installed open-air baths. Up through the early 1980s, indoor styles such as {{ill|Roman bath|ja|ローマ風呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Jungle bath|ja|ジャングル風呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were mainstream. However, around the late 1980s during the {{ill|Japanese asset price bubble|ja|バブル景気|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, rotenburo began to draw more attention. Sometimes, even a bath that has a ceiling and three walls (thus feeling nearly as enclosed as an indoor bath) might be marketed as a “rotenburo,” or described as “semi-open-air” (“半露天”). == Rotenburo Banzuke == '''Rotenburo Banzuke''' (露天風呂番付) is a form of {{ill|Onsen banzuke|ja|温泉番付|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ranking hot spring areas that are famous for their open-air baths. It was compiled in 1981, edited by '''Noguchi Fuyuto'''. In this Ranking, the Western {{ill|Yokozuna|ja|横綱|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the “Sunayu” at {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the Eastern Yokozuna is {{ill|Takaragawa Onsen|ja|宝川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. At Yubara Onsen, there is a large monument depicting this Rotenburo Banzuke, serving as a symbol of the hot spring resort. == Variations of Open-Air Baths == Yumura onsen20n4592.jpg|Open-air bath in a forest setting
({{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|2=ja|3=湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|4=ms|5=Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|6=pl|7=Yumura Onsen|lt=Yumura Onsen|qq=draft|lt=Yumura Onsen}}) Ryokukeitouen Kirishima City Kagoshima Pref09n4592.jpg|Open-air bath at a mountain stream
({{ill|Sakaenoo Onsen|ja|栄之尾温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) 190714 Settuskyo Onsen Takatsuki Osaka pref Japan01s3.jpg|Open-air bath with a gorge view
({{ill|Settsukyō Onsen|ja|摂津峡温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) Alpa Komakusa 2008.jpg|Open-air bath with a lake view (Tazawako Kōgen Onsen “Alpa Komakusa”) Tamatsukuri onsen yado01s3648.jpg|A guest room with an attached open-air bath
({{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) Koeda-Fudousan Katsura Koeda P6237036.JPG|A personal open-air bath built on private villa grounds == Management == [[File:新穂高の湯001.jpg|thumb|Cleaning an open-air bath
({{ill|Shinhodaka Onsen|ja|新穂高温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] From a management perspective, especially with a free-flowing hot spring of high source temperature, weather changes can significantly alter the bath’s temperature, requiring more precise temperature control. Additionally, due to the constant possibility of contamination by {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bacteria, stricter hygiene measures than those for indoor baths are neededJapan Association of Living and Environmental Health “[http://www.seiei.or.jp/db-cho/le-11.html Legionella Prevention Guidelines]” (Revised March 1999; accessed January 5, 2016).. == Other == [[File:Iceland - Blue Lagoon 09 (6571266721).jpg|thumb|{{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|qq=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] * The open-air bath “Edo-jōwa Yoichi” at {{ill|Spa Resort Hawaiians|ja|スパリゾートハワイアンズ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is listed in the “{{ill|Guinness World Records|ja|ギネス世界記録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2009” as the world’s largest open-air bath with a total tub area of 1000m²{{Cite web|和書|url=http://blog.hawaiians.co.jp/archives/50736632.html|title=世界一の大露天風呂|publisher=スパリゾートハワイアンズ|date=2008-11-08|accessdate=2021-06-03}}. * In {{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, there is an open-air spa called the {{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|qq=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon|qq=draft}} that covers about 5,000m² (with approximately 2,400m² open for general bathing)Iceland Tourism & Culture Research Institute “[http://www.iceland-kankobunka.jp/guide/reykjanes/blgn1.htm Tourist Spot Guide: Blue Lagoon]” (accessed January 5, 2016).. * {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is “Open-Air Bath Day” (a pun on “Ro (6) Ten (.) Bu (2) Ro (6)”). It was established on June 26, 1987, by {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Conceived in 1987 as part of a local revitalization project by young people in Yubara Onsen.[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html Official site of the 6.26 Open-Air Bath Day Committee] (Yubara Town Ryokan Cooperative). == Notes == == Sources == == Related topics == * {{ill|part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburoでの組織的盗撮事件|qq=draft|1=Organized voyeurism case in an open-air bath|3=露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件|1=Organized Voyeurism Scandal at Rotenburo baths|qq=draft}} == External links == * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm Open-Air Bath Banzuke Chart] {{Onsen-stub}} '''Rotenburo''' (露天風呂) {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs]] [[Category:Bathing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Rotenburo]] [[ja:露天風呂]] [[ja:%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82]] [[ko:%EB%85%B8%EC%B2%9C%ED%83%95]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rotenburo}}

revision.comment: 70 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:露天風呂 up to rev 102106783
revision.text:
[[ファイル:Onsen in Nachikatsuura, Japan.jpg|thumb|280px|海展望の露天風呂（[[南紀勝浦温泉]]）]] [[File:Iwojima Higashi onsen.jpg|280px|thumb|東シナ海を望む自噴泉「[[東温泉]]」（[[硫黄島 (鹿児島県)|薩摩硫黄島]]）]] '''露天風呂'''（ろてんぶろ）は、野外や屋外に設置された[[屋根]]や囲いを設けない[[風呂]]である[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82-663841 コトバンク - デジタル大辞泉(三省堂)]。'''野風呂'''（のぶろ）、'''野天風呂'''（のてんぶろ）とも呼ばれる野天風呂（野風呂）といった場合、[[野湯]]のように自然に湧き出たそのままの状態ではないが、露天風呂程には壁や屋根などが整備されていない状態を指す場合もある。。 == 概要 == 露天風呂は[[温泉]][[入浴]]の原[[風景]]とも言える。[[野湯]]のような状態であった温泉に[[浴槽]]を整備したものが始まりである。 温泉における露天風呂の人気は高い。景色を眺めながら入浴出来ることや、入浴時に開放感を味わえること、また浴室内に[[熱]]がこもるようなことがないためのぼせにくいことなど様々な要因が挙げられる。そのため、多くの[[旅館]]や[[日帰り入浴施設]]では[[内湯]]だけでなく露天風呂も併設している。 近年は旧来の温泉に限らず、多くの[[スーパー銭湯]]などにおいても露天風呂の設置がみられる。[[1980年代]]初めまではローマ風呂や[[ジャングル風呂]]に代表される屋内風呂が主流であったが、1980年代後半の[[バブル景気]]の頃より露天風呂が持て囃されるようになった。 なお、[[天井]]と三方の[[壁]]に囲まれ屋内とさほど変わりない閉塞感のある状況にもかかわらず「露天風呂」と称している事例や「半露天」などと称している事例もある。 == 露天風呂番付 == '''露天風呂番付'''（ろてんぶろばんづけ）は、[[温泉番付]]の一種であり、露天風呂が有名な[[温泉地]]を[[番付]]したものである。作成されたのは[[1981年]]で、[[野口冬人]]の手で編集された。露天風呂番付における西の[[横綱]]は[[湯原温泉]]の「砂湯」、東の横綱は[[水上温泉郷|宝川温泉]]である。湯原温泉には大きな露天風呂番付のモニュメントが掲示されており、温泉地の[[シンボル]]となっている。 == 露天風呂の変種 == Yumura onsen20n4592.jpg|森林露天風呂
（[[湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|湯村温泉]]） Ryokukeitouen Kirishima City Kagoshima Pref09n4592.jpg|渓流の露天風呂
（[[栄之尾温泉]]） 190714 Settuskyo Onsen Takatsuki Osaka pref Japan01s3.jpg|[[摂津峡|峡谷]]展望の露天風呂
（[[摂津峡温泉]]） Alpa Komakusa 2008.jpg|[[湖]]展望の露天風呂（田沢湖高原温泉アルパ駒草） Tamatsukuri onsen yado01s3648.jpg|客室付きの露天風呂
（[[玉造温泉]]） Koeda-Fudousan Katsura Koeda P6237036.JPG|別荘の敷地内に設けられた個人用露天風呂 == 管理 == [[ファイル:新穂高の湯001.jpg|thumb|露天風呂の清掃
（[[新穂高温泉]]）]] 管理上の問題として、特に源泉温度が高い掛け流し温泉では、湯温が天候状態によって大きく上下するため、より細やかな温度管理が必要となる。 また、常時[[レジオネラ|レジオネラ属菌]]によって汚染される機会にあるため、内湯以上の厳しい衛生管理が必要である[[全国生活衛生営業指導センター]]「[http://www.seiei.or.jp/db-cho/le-11.html レジオネラ属菌防止に関する防除指針]」1999年3月改定、2016年1月5日閲覧。。 == その他 == [[ファイル:Iceland - Blue Lagoon 09 (6571266721).jpg|thumb|[[ブルーラグーン]]（[[アイスランド]]）]] * [[福島県]][[いわき市]]の[[スパリゾートハワイアンズ]]内にある露天風呂「江戸情話 与市」は、総浴槽面積1000㎡の世界一広い露天風呂として「[[ギネス世界記録]]2009」に登録されている{{Cite web|和書|url=http://blog.hawaiians.co.jp/archives/50736632.html|title=世界一の大露天風呂|publisher=スパリゾートハワイアンズ|date=2008-11-08|accessdate=2021-06-03}}。 * [[アイスランド]]には広さ5,000m2（うち一般の入浴が可能な範囲は約2,400m2）の露天風呂「[[ブルーラグーン]]」があるアイスランド観光文化研究所「[http://www.iceland-kankobunka.jp/guide/reykjanes/blgn1.htm ツーリスト・スポット徹底ガイド ブルーラグーン]」2016年1月5日閲覧。。 * [[6月26日]]は露天風呂の日（「ろ（6）てん（・）ぶ（2）ろ（6）」という[[語呂合わせ]]）。[[1987年]]6月26日に[[岡山県]]の[[湯原温泉]]が制定湯原温泉の若者達のアイデアによる「町づくり事業」として、[[1987年]]に始められた。[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html 6・26 露天風呂の日 実行委員会公式ページ]（湯原町旅館協同組合）。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === === 出典 === == 関連項目 == * [[露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Outdoor hot spring baths}} * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm 露天風呂番付表] {{温泉}} {{Onsen-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:ろてんふろ}} [[Category:温泉]] [[Category:入浴]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[露天風呂]] to [[Rotenburo]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
[[ファイル:Onsen in Nachikatsuura, Japan.jpg|thumb|280px|海展望の露天風呂（[[南紀勝浦温泉]]）]] [[File:Iwojima Higashi onsen.jpg|280px|thumb|東シナ海を望む自噴泉「[[東温泉]]」（[[硫黄島 (鹿児島県)|薩摩硫黄島]]）]] '''露天風呂'''（ろてんぶろ）は、野外や屋外に設置された[[屋根]]や囲いを設けない[[風呂]]である[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82-663841 コトバンク - デジタル大辞泉(三省堂)]。'''野風呂'''（のぶろ）、'''野天風呂'''（のてんぶろ）とも呼ばれる野天風呂（野風呂）といった場合、[[野湯]]のように自然に湧き出たそのままの状態ではないが、露天風呂程には壁や屋根などが整備されていない状態を指す場合もある。。 == 概要 == 露天風呂は[[温泉]][[入浴]]の原[[風景]]とも言える。[[野湯]]のような状態であった温泉に[[浴槽]]を整備したものが始まりである。 温泉における露天風呂の人気は高い。景色を眺めながら入浴出来ることや、入浴時に開放感を味わえること、また浴室内に[[熱]]がこもるようなことがないためのぼせにくいことなど様々な要因が挙げられる。そのため、多くの[[旅館]]や[[日帰り入浴施設]]では[[内湯]]だけでなく露天風呂も併設している。 近年は旧来の温泉に限らず、多くの[[スーパー銭湯]]などにおいても露天風呂の設置がみられる。[[1980年代]]初めまではローマ風呂や[[ジャングル風呂]]に代表される屋内風呂が主流であったが、1980年代後半の[[バブル景気]]の頃より露天風呂が持て囃されるようになった。 なお、[[天井]]と三方の[[壁]]に囲まれ屋内とさほど変わりない閉塞感のある状況にもかかわらず「露天風呂」と称している事例や「半露天」などと称している事例もある。 == 露天風呂番付 == '''露天風呂番付'''（ろてんぶろばんづけ）は、[[温泉番付]]の一種であり、露天風呂が有名な[[温泉地]]を[[番付]]したものである。作成されたのは[[1981年]]で、[[野口冬人]]の手で編集された。露天風呂番付における西の[[横綱]]は[[湯原温泉]]の「砂湯」、東の横綱は[[水上温泉郷|宝川温泉]]である。湯原温泉には大きな露天風呂番付のモニュメントが掲示されており、温泉地の[[シンボル]]となっている。 == 露天風呂の変種 == Yumura onsen20n4592.jpg|森林露天風呂
（[[湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|湯村温泉]]） Ryokukeitouen Kirishima City Kagoshima Pref09n4592.jpg|渓流の露天風呂
（[[栄之尾温泉]]） 190714 Settuskyo Onsen Takatsuki Osaka pref Japan01s3.jpg|[[摂津峡|峡谷]]展望の露天風呂
（[[摂津峡温泉]]） Alpa Komakusa 2008.jpg|[[湖]]展望の露天風呂（田沢湖高原温泉アルパ駒草） Tamatsukuri onsen yado01s3648.jpg|客室付きの露天風呂
（[[玉造温泉]]） Koeda-Fudousan Katsura Koeda P6237036.JPG|別荘の敷地内に設けられた個人用露天風呂 == 管理 == [[ファイル:新穂高の湯001.jpg|thumb|露天風呂の清掃
（[[新穂高温泉]]）]] 管理上の問題として、特に源泉温度が高い掛け流し温泉では、湯温が天候状態によって大きく上下するため、より細やかな温度管理が必要となる。 また、常時[[レジオネラ|レジオネラ属菌]]によって汚染される機会にあるため、内湯以上の厳しい衛生管理が必要である[[全国生活衛生営業指導センター]]「[http://www.seiei.or.jp/db-cho/le-11.html レジオネラ属菌防止に関する防除指針]」1999年3月改定、2016年1月5日閲覧。。 == その他 == [[ファイル:Iceland - Blue Lagoon 09 (6571266721).jpg|thumb|[[ブルーラグーン]]（[[アイスランド]]）]] * [[福島県]][[いわき市]]の[[スパリゾートハワイアンズ]]内にある露天風呂「江戸情話 与市」は、総浴槽面積1000㎡の世界一広い露天風呂として「[[ギネス世界記録]]2009」に登録されている{{Cite web|和書|url=http://blog.hawaiians.co.jp/archives/50736632.html|title=世界一の大露天風呂|publisher=スパリゾートハワイアンズ|date=2008-11-08|accessdate=2021-06-03}}。 * [[アイスランド]]には広さ5,000m2（うち一般の入浴が可能な範囲は約2,400m2）の露天風呂「[[ブルーラグーン]]」があるアイスランド観光文化研究所「[http://www.iceland-kankobunka.jp/guide/reykjanes/blgn1.htm ツーリスト・スポット徹底ガイド ブルーラグーン]」2016年1月5日閲覧。。 * [[6月26日]]は露天風呂の日（「ろ（6）てん（・）ぶ（2）ろ（6）」という[[語呂合わせ]]）。[[1987年]]6月26日に[[岡山県]]の[[湯原温泉]]が制定湯原温泉の若者達のアイデアによる「町づくり事業」として、[[1987年]]に始められた。[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html 6・26 露天風呂の日 実行委員会公式ページ]（湯原町旅館協同組合）。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === === 出典 === == 関連項目 == * [[露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Outdoor hot spring baths}} * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm 露天風呂番付表] {{温泉}} {{Onsen-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:ろてんふろ}} [[Category:温泉]] [[Category:入浴]]

{{afc comment|1={{ill|Sotoyu|ja|外湯|qq=draft|qq=draft}} refers to communal baths outside lodging facilities shared by multiple users, {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|qq=draft|qq=draft}} denotes in-house hot spring baths privately installed within lodging facilities, and {{ill|Rotenburo|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburo|qq=draft|qq=draft}} describes open-air baths typically without a roof or full enclosure.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:41, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Onsen in Nachikatsuura, Japan.jpg|thumb|280px|An open-air bath overlooking the sea ({{ill|Nanki-Katsuura Onsen|ja|南紀勝浦温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] [[File:Iwojima Higashi onsen.jpg|280px|thumb|A self-flowing hot spring “{{ill|Higashi Onsen (Iōjima)|ja|東温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}” facing the East China Sea ({{ill|Satsuma Iōjima|ja|硫黄島 (鹿児島県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] '''Rotenburo''' (open-air bath) is a type of {{ill|Bath|ja|風呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} built outdoors with no roof or enclosure[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82-663841 Kotobank - Digital Daijisen (Sanseidō)]. It is also called '''Noburo''' (野風呂) or '''Notenburo''' (野天風呂)When referred to as 野天風呂 (or 野風呂), it may mean a bath that is not entirely in its natural state like a {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|qq=draft|qq=draft}} but lacks as much development in walls or roofing as a typical open-air bath.. == Overview == Open-air baths can be considered the original “landscape” of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bathing. They began as naturally emerging hot springs like {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in which a simple bathing area (bathtub) was arranged. Rotenburo (open-air baths) are highly popular at hot spring resorts. Reasons include being able to enjoy scenic views while bathing, experiencing a sense of openness, and having less risk of overheating (since heat does not accumulate as much as in an indoor setting). As a result, many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|旅館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Day-use hot spring facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|qq=draft|qq=draft}} offer open-air baths in addition to {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In recent years, not only traditional onsen but also many {{ill|Supersento|ja|スーパー銭湯|1=Super Sento|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have installed open-air baths. Up through the early 1980s, indoor styles such as {{ill|Roman bath|ja|ローマ風呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Jungle bath|ja|ジャングル風呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were mainstream. However, around the late 1980s during the {{ill|Japanese asset price bubble|ja|バブル景気|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, rotenburo began to draw more attention. Sometimes, even a bath that has a ceiling and three walls (thus feeling nearly as enclosed as an indoor bath) might be marketed as a “rotenburo,” or described as “semi-open-air” (“半露天”). == Rotenburo Banzuke == '''Rotenburo Banzuke''' (露天風呂番付) is a form of {{ill|Onsen banzuke|ja|温泉番付|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ranking hot spring areas that are famous for their open-air baths. It was compiled in 1981, edited by '''Noguchi Fuyuto'''. In this Ranking, the Western {{ill|Yokozuna|ja|横綱|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the “Sunayu” at {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the Eastern Yokozuna is {{ill|Takaragawa Onsen|ja|宝川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. At Yubara Onsen, there is a large monument depicting this Rotenburo Banzuke, serving as a symbol of the hot spring resort. == Variations of Open-Air Baths == Yumura onsen20n4592.jpg|Open-air bath in a forest setting
({{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|2=ja|3=湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|4=ms|5=Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|6=pl|7=Yumura Onsen|lt=Yumura Onsen|qq=draft|lt=Yumura Onsen}}) Ryokukeitouen Kirishima City Kagoshima Pref09n4592.jpg|Open-air bath at a mountain stream
({{ill|Sakaenoo Onsen|ja|栄之尾温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) 190714 Settuskyo Onsen Takatsuki Osaka pref Japan01s3.jpg|Open-air bath with a gorge view
({{ill|Settsukyō Onsen|ja|摂津峡温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) Alpa Komakusa 2008.jpg|Open-air bath with a lake view (Tazawako Kōgen Onsen “Alpa Komakusa”) Tamatsukuri onsen yado01s3648.jpg|A guest room with an attached open-air bath
({{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) Koeda-Fudousan Katsura Koeda P6237036.JPG|A personal open-air bath built on private villa grounds == Management == [[File:新穂高の湯001.jpg|thumb|Cleaning an open-air bath
({{ill|Shinhodaka Onsen|ja|新穂高温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] From a management perspective, especially with a free-flowing hot spring of high source temperature, weather changes can significantly alter the bath’s temperature, requiring more precise temperature control. Additionally, due to the constant possibility of contamination by {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bacteria, stricter hygiene measures than those for indoor baths are neededJapan Association of Living and Environmental Health “[http://www.seiei.or.jp/db-cho/le-11.html Legionella Prevention Guidelines]” (Revised March 1999; accessed January 5, 2016).. == Other == [[File:Iceland - Blue Lagoon 09 (6571266721).jpg|thumb|{{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|qq=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] * The open-air bath “Edo-jōwa Yoichi” at {{ill|Spa Resort Hawaiians|ja|スパリゾートハワイアンズ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is listed in the “{{ill|Guinness World Records|ja|ギネス世界記録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2009” as the world’s largest open-air bath with a total tub area of 1000m²{{Cite web|和書|url=http://blog.hawaiians.co.jp/archives/50736632.html|title=世界一の大露天風呂|publisher=スパリゾートハワイアンズ|date=2008-11-08|accessdate=2021-06-03}}. * In {{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, there is an open-air spa called the {{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|qq=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon|qq=draft}} that covers about 5,000m² (with approximately 2,400m² open for general bathing)Iceland Tourism & Culture Research Institute “[http://www.iceland-kankobunka.jp/guide/reykjanes/blgn1.htm Tourist Spot Guide: Blue Lagoon]” (accessed January 5, 2016).. * {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is “Open-Air Bath Day” (a pun on “Ro (6) Ten (.) Bu (2) Ro (6)”). It was established on June 26, 1987, by {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Conceived in 1987 as part of a local revitalization project by young people in Yubara Onsen.[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html Official site of the 6.26 Open-Air Bath Day Committee] (Yubara Town Ryokan Cooperative). == Notes == == Sources == == Related topics == * {{ill|part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburoでの組織的盗撮事件|qq=draft|1=Organized voyeurism case in an open-air bath|3=露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件|1=Organized Voyeurism Scandal at Rotenburo baths|qq=draft}} == External links == * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm Open-Air Bath Banzuke Chart] {{Onsen-stub}} '''Rotenburo''' (露天風呂) {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs]] [[Category:Bathing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Rotenburo]] [[ja:露天風呂]] [[ja:%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82]] [[ko:%EB%85%B8%EC%B2%9C%ED%83%95]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rotenburo}} {{translated page|ja|露天風呂|version=102106783|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Up through the early 1980s, indoor styles such as {{ill|Roman bath|ja|ローマ風呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Jungle bath|ja|ジャングル風呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were mainstream. However, around the late 1980s during the {{ill|Japanese asset price bubble|ja|バブル景気|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, rotenburo began to draw more attention. Sometimes, even a bath that has a ceiling and three walls (thus feeling nearly as enclosed as an indoor bath) might be marketed as a “rotenburo,” or described as “semi-open-air” (“半露天”). == Rotenburo Banzuke == '''Rotenburo Banzuke''' (露天風呂番付) is a form of {{ill|Onsen banzuke|ja|温泉番付|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ranking hot spring areas that are famous for their open-air baths. It was compiled in 1981, edited by '''Noguchi Fuyuto'''. In this Ranking, the Western {{ill|Yokozuna|ja|横綱|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the “Sunayu” at {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the Eastern Yokozuna is {{ill|Takaragawa Onsen|ja|宝川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. At Yubara Onsen, there is a large monument depicting this Rotenburo Banzuke, serving as a symbol of the hot spring resort. == Variations of Open-Air Baths == Yumura onsen20n4592.jpg|Open-air bath in a forest setting
({{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|2=ja|3=湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|4=ms|5=Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|6=pl|7=Yumura Onsen|lt=Yumura Onsen|qq=draft|lt=Yumura Onsen}}) Ryokukeitouen Kirishima City Kagoshima Pref09n4592.jpg|Open-air bath at a mountain stream
({{ill|Sakaenoo Onsen|ja|栄之尾温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) 190714 Settuskyo Onsen Takatsuki Osaka pref Japan01s3.jpg|Open-air bath with a gorge view
({{ill|Settsukyō Onsen|ja|摂津峡温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) Alpa Komakusa 2008.jpg|Open-air bath with a lake view (Tazawako Kōgen Onsen “Alpa Komakusa”) Tamatsukuri onsen yado01s3648.jpg|A guest room with an attached open-air bath
({{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) Koeda-Fudousan Katsura Koeda P6237036.JPG|A personal open-air bath built on private villa grounds == Management == [[File:新穂高の湯001.jpg|thumb|Cleaning an open-air bath
({{ill|Shinhodaka Onsen|ja|新穂高温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] From a management perspective, especially with a free-flowing hot spring of high source temperature, weather changes can significantly alter the bath’s temperature, requiring more precise temperature control. Additionally, due to the constant possibility of contamination by {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bacteria, stricter hygiene measures than those for indoor baths are neededJapan Association of Living and Environmental Health “[http://www.seiei.or.jp/db-cho/le-11.html Legionella Prevention Guidelines]” (Revised March 1999; accessed January 5, 2016).. == Other == [[File:Iceland - Blue Lagoon 09 (6571266721).jpg|thumb|{{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|qq=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] * The open-air bath “Edo-jōwa Yoichi” at {{ill|Spa Resort Hawaiians|ja|スパリゾートハワイアンズ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is listed in the “{{ill|Guinness World Records|ja|ギネス世界記録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2009” as the world’s largest open-air bath with a total tub area of 1000m²{{Cite web|和書|url=http://blog.hawaiians.co.jp/archives/50736632.html|title=世界一の大露天風呂|publisher=スパリゾートハワイアンズ|date=2008-11-08|accessdate=2021-06-03}}. * In {{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, there is an open-air spa called the {{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|qq=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon|qq=draft}} that covers about 5,000m² (with approximately 2,400m² open for general bathing)Iceland Tourism & Culture Research Institute “[http://www.iceland-kankobunka.jp/guide/reykjanes/blgn1.htm Tourist Spot Guide: Blue Lagoon]” (accessed January 5, 2016).. * {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is “Open-Air Bath Day” (a pun on “Ro (6) Ten (.) Bu (2) Ro (6)”). It was established on June 26, 1987, by {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Conceived in 1987 as part of a local revitalization project by young people in Yubara Onsen.[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html Official site of the 6.26 Open-Air Bath Day Committee] (Yubara Town Ryokan Cooperative). == Notes == == Sources == == Related topics == * {{ill|part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburoでの組織的盗撮事件|qq=draft|1=Organized voyeurism case in an open-air bath|3=露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件|1=Organized Voyeurism Scandal at Rotenburo baths|qq=draft}} == External links == * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm Open-Air Bath Banzuke Chart] {{Onsen-stub}} '''Rotenburo''' (露天風呂) {{Onsen}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Rotenburo}} {{translated page|ja|露天風呂|version=102106783|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs]] [[Category:Bathing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Rotenburo]] [[ja:露天風呂]] [[ja:%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82]] [[ko:%EB%85%B8%EC%B2%9C%ED%83%95]]

{{afc comment|1={{ill|Sotoyu|ja|外湯|qq=draft|qq=draft}} refers to communal baths outside lodging facilities shared by multiple users, {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|qq=draft|qq=draft}} denotes in-house hot spring baths privately installed within lodging facilities, and {{ill|Rotenburo|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburo|qq=draft|qq=draft}} describes open-air baths typically without a roof or full enclosure.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:41, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Onsen in Nachikatsuura, Japan.jpg|thumb|280px|An open-air bath overlooking the sea ({{ill|Nanki-Katsuura Onsen|ja|南紀勝浦温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] [[File:Iwojima Higashi onsen.jpg|280px|thumb|A self-flowing hot spring “{{ill|Higashi Onsen (Iōjima)|ja|東温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}” facing the East China Sea ({{ill|Satsuma Iōjima|ja|硫黄島 (鹿児島県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] '''Rotenburo''' (open-air bath) is a type of {{ill|Bath|ja|風呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} built outdoors with no roof or enclosure[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82-663841 Kotobank - Digital Daijisen (Sanseidō)]. It is also called '''Noburo''' (野風呂) or '''Notenburo''' (野天風呂)When referred to as 野天風呂 (or 野風呂), it may mean a bath that is not entirely in its natural state like a {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|qq=draft|qq=draft}} but lacks as much development in walls or roofing as a typical open-air bath.. == Overview == Open-air baths can be considered the original “landscape” of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bathing. They began as naturally emerging hot springs like {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in which a simple bathing area (bathtub) was arranged. Rotenburo (open-air baths) are highly popular at hot spring resorts. Reasons include being able to enjoy scenic views while bathing, experiencing a sense of openness, and having less risk of overheating (since heat does not accumulate as much as in an indoor setting). As a result, many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|旅館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Day-use hot spring facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|qq=draft|qq=draft}} offer open-air baths in addition to {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In recent years, not only traditional onsen but also many {{ill|Supersento|ja|スーパー銭湯|1=Super Sento|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have installed open-air baths. Up through the early 1980s, indoor styles such as {{ill|Roman bath|ja|ローマ風呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Jungle bath|ja|ジャングル風呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were mainstream. However, around the late 1980s during the {{ill|Japanese asset price bubble|ja|バブル景気|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, rotenburo began to draw more attention. Sometimes, even a bath that has a ceiling and three walls (thus feeling nearly as enclosed as an indoor bath) might be marketed as a “rotenburo,” or described as “semi-open-air” (“半露天”). == Rotenburo Banzuke == '''Rotenburo Banzuke''' (露天風呂番付) is a form of {{ill|Onsen banzuke|ja|温泉番付|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ranking hot spring areas that are famous for their open-air baths. It was compiled in 1981, edited by '''Noguchi Fuyuto'''. In this Ranking, the Western {{ill|Yokozuna|ja|横綱|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the “Sunayu” at {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the Eastern Yokozuna is {{ill|Takaragawa Onsen|ja|宝川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. At Yubara Onsen, there is a large monument depicting this Rotenburo Banzuke, serving as a symbol of the hot spring resort. == Variations of Open-Air Baths == Yumura onsen20n4592.jpg|Open-air bath in a forest setting
({{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|2=ja|3=湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|4=ms|5=Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|6=pl|7=Yumura Onsen|lt=Yumura Onsen|qq=draft|lt=Yumura Onsen}}) Ryokukeitouen Kirishima City Kagoshima Pref09n4592.jpg|Open-air bath at a mountain stream
({{ill|Sakaenoo Onsen|ja|栄之尾温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) 190714 Settuskyo Onsen Takatsuki Osaka pref Japan01s3.jpg|Open-air bath with a gorge view
({{ill|Settsukyō Onsen|ja|摂津峡温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) Alpa Komakusa 2008.jpg|Open-air bath with a lake view (Tazawako Kōgen Onsen “Alpa Komakusa”) Tamatsukuri onsen yado01s3648.jpg|A guest room with an attached open-air bath
({{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) Koeda-Fudousan Katsura Koeda P6237036.JPG|A personal open-air bath built on private villa grounds == Management == [[File:新穂高の湯001.jpg|thumb|Cleaning an open-air bath
({{ill|Shinhodaka Onsen|ja|新穂高温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] From a management perspective, especially with a free-flowing hot spring of high source temperature, weather changes can significantly alter the bath’s temperature, requiring more precise temperature control. Additionally, due to the constant possibility of contamination by {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bacteria, stricter hygiene measures than those for indoor baths are neededJapan Association of Living and Environmental Health “[http://www.seiei.or.jp/db-cho/le-11.html Legionella Prevention Guidelines]” (Revised March 1999; accessed January 5, 2016).. == Other == [[File:Iceland - Blue Lagoon 09 (6571266721).jpg|thumb|{{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|qq=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] * The open-air bath “Edo-jōwa Yoichi” at {{ill|Spa Resort Hawaiians|ja|スパリゾートハワイアンズ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is listed in the “{{ill|Guinness World Records|ja|ギネス世界記録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2009” as the world’s largest open-air bath with a total tub area of 1000m²{{Cite web|和書|url=http://blog.hawaiians.co.jp/archives/50736632.html|title=世界一の大露天風呂|publisher=スパリゾートハワイアンズ|date=2008-11-08|accessdate=2021-06-03}}. * In {{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, there is an open-air spa called the {{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|qq=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon|qq=draft}} that covers about 5,000m² (with approximately 2,400m² open for general bathing)Iceland Tourism & Culture Research Institute “[http://www.iceland-kankobunka.jp/guide/reykjanes/blgn1.htm Tourist Spot Guide: Blue Lagoon]” (accessed January 5, 2016).. * {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is “Open-Air Bath Day” (a pun on “Ro (6) Ten (.) Bu (2) Ro (6)”). It was established on June 26, 1987, by {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Conceived in 1987 as part of a local revitalization project by young people in Yubara Onsen.[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html Official site of the 6.26 Open-Air Bath Day Committee] (Yubara Town Ryokan Cooperative). == Notes == == Sources == == Related topics == * {{ill|part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburoでの組織的盗撮事件|qq=draft|1=Organized voyeurism case in an open-air bath|3=露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件|1=Organized Voyeurism Scandal at Rotenburo baths|qq=draft}} == External links == * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm Open-Air Bath Banzuke Chart] {{Onsen-stub}} '''Rotenburo''' (露天風呂) {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|露天風呂|version=102106783|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs]] [[Category:Bathing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Rotenburo]] [[ja:露天風呂]] [[ja:%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82]] [[ko:%EB%85%B8%EC%B2%9C%ED%83%95]]

Bu (2) Ro (6)”). It was established on June 26, 1987, by {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Conceived in 1987 as part of a local revitalization project by young people in Yubara Onsen.[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html Official site of the 6.26 Open-Air Bath Day Committee] (Yubara Town Ryokan Cooperative). == Notes == == Sources == == See Also == * {{ill|part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburoでの組織的盗撮事件|qq=draft|1=Organized voyeurism case in an open-air bath|3=露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件|1=Organized Voyeurism Scandal at Rotenburo baths|qq=draft}} == External links == * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm Open-Air Bath Banzuke Chart] {{Onsen-stub}} '''Rotenburo''' (露天風呂) {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|露天風呂|version=102106783|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs]] [[Category:Bathing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Rotenburo]] [[ja:露天風呂]] [[ja:%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82]] [[ko:%EB%85%B8%EC%B2%9C%ED%83%95]]

{{afc comment|1={{ill|Sotoyu|ja|外湯|qq=draft|qq=draft}} refers to communal baths outside lodging facilities shared by multiple users, {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|qq=draft|qq=draft}} denotes in-house hot spring baths privately installed within lodging facilities, and {{ill|Rotenburo|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburo|qq=draft|qq=draft}} describes open-air baths typically without a roof or full enclosure.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:41, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Onsen in Nachikatsuura, Japan.jpg|thumb|280px|An open-air bath overlooking the sea ({{ill|Nanki-Katsuura Onsen|ja|南紀勝浦温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] [[File:Iwojima Higashi onsen.jpg|280px|thumb|A self-flowing hot spring “{{ill|Higashi Onsen (Iōjima)|ja|東温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}” facing the East China Sea ({{ill|Satsuma Iōjima|ja|硫黄島 (鹿児島県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] '''Rotenburo''' (open-air bath) is a type of {{ill|Bath|ja|風呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} built outdoors with no roof or enclosure[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82-663841 Kotobank - Digital Daijisen (Sanseidō)]. It is also called '''Noburo''' (野風呂) or '''Notenburo''' (野天風呂)When referred to as 野天風呂 (or 野風呂), it may mean a bath that is not entirely in its natural state like a {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|qq=draft|qq=draft}} but lacks as much development in walls or roofing as a typical open-air bath.. == Overview == Open-air baths can be considered the original “landscape” of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bathing. They began as naturally emerging hot springs like {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in which a simple bathing area (bathtub) was arranged. Rotenburo (open-air baths) are highly popular at hot spring resorts. Reasons include being able to enjoy scenic views while bathing, experiencing a sense of openness, and having less risk of overheating (since heat does not accumulate as much as in an indoor setting). As a result, many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|旅館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Day-use hot spring facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|qq=draft|qq=draft}} offer open-air baths in addition to {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In recent years, not only traditional onsen but also many {{ill|Supersento|ja|スーパー銭湯|1=Super Sento|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have installed open-air baths. Up through the early 1980s, indoor styles such as {{ill|Roman bath|ja|ローマ風呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Jungle bath|ja|ジャングル風呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were mainstream. However, around the late 1980s during the {{ill|Japanese asset price bubble|ja|バブル景気|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, rotenburo began to draw more attention. Sometimes, even a bath that has a ceiling and three walls (thus feeling nearly as enclosed as an indoor bath) might be marketed as a “rotenburo,” or described as “semi-open-air” (“半露天”). == Rotenburo Banzuke == '''Rotenburo Banzuke''' (露天風呂番付) is a form of {{ill|Onsen banzuke|ja|温泉番付|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ranking hot spring areas that are famous for their open-air baths. It was compiled in 1981, edited by '''Noguchi Fuyuto'''. In this Ranking, the Western {{ill|Yokozuna|ja|横綱|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the “Sunayu” at {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the Eastern Yokozuna is {{ill|Takaragawa Onsen|ja|宝川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. At Yubara Onsen, there is a large monument depicting this Rotenburo Banzuke, serving as a symbol of the hot spring resort. == Variations of Open-Air Baths == Yumura onsen20n4592.jpg|Open-air bath in a forest setting
({{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|2=ja|3=湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|4=ms|5=Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|6=pl|7=Yumura Onsen|lt=Yumura Onsen|qq=draft|lt=Yumura Onsen}}) Ryokukeitouen Kirishima City Kagoshima Pref09n4592.jpg|Open-air bath at a mountain stream
({{ill|Sakaenoo Onsen|ja|栄之尾温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) 190714 Settuskyo Onsen Takatsuki Osaka pref Japan01s3.jpg|Open-air bath with a gorge view
({{ill|Settsukyō Onsen|ja|摂津峡温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) Alpa Komakusa 2008.jpg|Open-air bath with a lake view (Tazawako Kōgen Onsen “Alpa Komakusa”) Tamatsukuri onsen yado01s3648.jpg|A guest room with an attached open-air bath
({{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) Koeda-Fudousan Katsura Koeda P6237036.JPG|A personal open-air bath built on private villa grounds == Management == [[File:新穂高の湯001.jpg|thumb|Cleaning an open-air bath
({{ill|Shinhodaka Onsen|ja|新穂高温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] From a management perspective, especially with a free-flowing hot spring of high source temperature, weather changes can significantly alter the bath’s temperature, requiring more precise temperature control. Additionally, due to the constant possibility of contamination by {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bacteria, stricter hygiene measures than those for indoor baths are neededJapan Association of Living and Environmental Health “[http://www.seiei.or.jp/db-cho/le-11.html Legionella Prevention Guidelines]” (Revised March 1999; accessed January 5, 2016).. == Other == [[File:Iceland - Blue Lagoon 09 (6571266721).jpg|thumb|{{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|qq=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] * The open-air bath “Edo-jōwa Yoichi” at {{ill|Spa Resort Hawaiians|ja|スパリゾートハワイアンズ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is listed in the “{{ill|Guinness World Records|ja|ギネス世界記録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2009” as the world’s largest open-air bath with a total tub area of 1000m²{{Cite web|和書|url=http://blog.hawaiians.co.jp/archives/50736632.html|title=世界一の大露天風呂|publisher=スパリゾートハワイアンズ|date=2008-11-08|accessdate=2021-06-03}}. * In {{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, there is an open-air spa called the {{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|qq=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon|qq=draft}} that covers about 5,000m² (with approximately 2,400m² open for general bathing)Iceland Tourism & Culture Research Institute “[http://www.iceland-kankobunka.jp/guide/reykjanes/blgn1.htm Tourist Spot Guide: Blue Lagoon]” (accessed January 5, 2016).. * {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is “Open-Air Bath Day” (a pun on “Ro (6) Ten (.) Bu (2) Ro (6)”). It was established on June 26, 1987, by {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Conceived in 1987 as part of a local revitalization project by young people in Yubara Onsen.[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html Official site of the 6.26 Open-Air Bath Day Committee] (Yubara Town Ryokan Cooperative). == Notes == == Sources == == See Also == * {{ill|part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburoでの組織的盗撮事件|qq=draft|1=Organized voyeurism case in an open-air bath|3=露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件|1=Organized Voyeurism Scandal at Rotenburo baths|qq=draft}} == External links == * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm Open-Air Bath Banzuke Chart] {{Onsen-stub}} '''Rotenburo''' (露天風呂) {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|露天風呂|version=102106783|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Bathing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Rotenburo]] [[ja:露天風呂]] [[ja:%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82]] [[ko:%EB%85%B8%EC%B2%9C%ED%83%95]]

Rotenburo (open-air baths) are highly popular at hot spring resorts. Sometimes, even a bath that has a ceiling and three walls (thus feeling nearly as enclosed as an indoor bath) might be marketed as a “rotenburo,” or described as “semi-open-air” (“半露天”). == Rotenburo Banzuke == '''Rotenburo Banzuke''' (露天風呂番付) is a form of {{ill|Onsen banzuke|ja|温泉番付|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen banzuke|qq=}}, Ranking hot spring areas that are famous for their open-air baths. It was compiled in 1981, edited by '''Noguchi Fuyuto'''. In this Ranking, the Western {{ill|Yokozuna|ja|横綱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokozuna|qq=}} is the “Sunayu” at {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|qq=}}, and the Eastern Yokozuna is {{ill|Takaragawa Onsen|ja|宝川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaragawa Onsen|qq=}}. At Yubara Onsen, there is a large monument depicting this Rotenburo Banzuke, serving as a symbol of the hot spring resort. == Variations of Open-Air Baths == Yumura onsen20n4592.jpg|Open-air bath in a forest setting
({{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|2=ja|3=湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|4=ms|5=Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|6=pl|7=Yumura Onsen|lt=Yumura Onsen|qq=draft|lt=Yumura Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|qq=}}) Ryokukeitouen Kirishima City Kagoshima Pref09n4592.jpg|Open-air bath at a mountain stream
({{ill|Sakaenoo Onsen|ja|栄之尾温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaenoo Onsen|qq=}}) 190714 Settuskyo Onsen Takatsuki Osaka pref Japan01s3.jpg|Open-air bath with a gorge view
({{ill|Settsukyō Onsen|ja|摂津峡温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsukyō Onsen|qq=}}) Alpa Komakusa 2008.jpg|Open-air bath with a lake view (Tazawako Kōgen Onsen “Alpa Komakusa”) Tamatsukuri onsen yado01s3648.jpg|A guest room with an attached open-air bath
({{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsukuri Onsen|qq=}}) Koeda-Fudousan Katsura Koeda P6237036.JPG|A personal open-air bath built on private villa grounds == Management == [[File:新穂高の湯001.jpg|thumb|Cleaning an open-air bath
({{ill|Shinhodaka Onsen|ja|新穂高温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinhodaka Onsen|qq=}})]] From a management perspective, especially with a free-flowing hot spring of high source temperature, weather changes can significantly alter the bath’s temperature, requiring more precise temperature control. Additionally, due to the constant possibility of contamination by {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legionella|qq=}} bacteria, stricter hygiene measures than those for indoor baths are neededJapan Association of Living and Environmental Health “[http://www.seiei.or.jp/db-cho/le-11.html Legionella Prevention Guidelines]” (Revised March 1999; accessed January 5, 2016).. == Other == [[File:Iceland - Blue Lagoon 09 (6571266721).jpg|thumb|{{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|qq=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|qq=}} ({{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iceland|qq=}})]] * The open-air bath “Edo-jōwa Yoichi” at {{ill|Spa Resort Hawaiians|ja|スパリゾートハワイアンズ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spa Resort Hawaiians|qq=}} in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki, Fukushima|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}} is listed in the “{{ill|Guinness World Records|ja|ギネス世界記録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guinness World Records|qq=}} 2009” as the world’s largest open-air bath with a total tub area of 1000m²{{Cite web|和書|url=http://blog.hawaiians.co.jp/archives/50736632.html|title=世界一の大露天風呂|publisher=スパリゾートハワイアンズ|date=2008-11-08|accessdate=2021-06-03}}. * In {{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iceland|qq=}}, there is an open-air spa called the {{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|qq=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|qq=}} that covers about 5,000m² (with approximately 2,400m² open for general bathing)Iceland Tourism & Culture Research Institute “[http://www.iceland-kankobunka.jp/guide/reykjanes/blgn1.htm Tourist Spot Guide: Blue Lagoon]” (accessed January 5, 2016).. * {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26|qq=}} is “Open-Air Bath Day” (a pun on “Ro (6) Ten (.) Bu (2) Ro (6)”). It was established on June 26, 1987, by {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|qq=}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}Conceived in 1987 as part of a local revitalization project by young people in Yubara Onsen.[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html Official site of the 6.26 Open-Air Bath Day Committee] (Yubara Town Ryokan Cooperative). == Notes == == Sources == == See Also == * {{ill|part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburoでの組織的盗撮事件|qq=draft|1=Organized voyeurism case in an open-air bath|3=露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件|1=Organized Voyeurism Scandal at Rotenburo baths|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotenburo|qq=}} == External links == * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm Open-Air Bath Banzuke Chart] {{Onsen-stub}} '''Rotenburo''' (露天風呂) {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|露天風呂|version=102106783|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Bathing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Rotenburo]] [[ja:露天風呂]] [[ja:%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82]] [[ko:%EB%85%B8%EC%B2%9C%ED%83%95]]

{{afc comment|1={{ill|Sotoyu|ja|外湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotoyu|qq=}} refers to communal baths outside lodging facilities shared by multiple users, {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiyu|qq=}} denotes in-house hot spring baths privately installed within lodging facilities, and {{ill|Rotenburo|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotenburo|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotenburo|qq=}} describes open-air baths typically without a roof or full enclosure.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:41, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Onsen in Nachikatsuura, Japan.jpg|thumb|280px|An open-air bath overlooking the sea ({{ill|Nanki-Katsuura Onsen|ja|南紀勝浦温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanki-Katsuura Onsen|qq=}})]] [[File:Iwojima Higashi onsen.jpg|280px|thumb|A self-flowing hot spring “{{ill|Higashi Onsen (Iōjima)|ja|東温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Onsen (Iōjima)|qq=}}” facing the East China Sea ({{ill|Satsuma Iōjima|ja|硫黄島 (鹿児島県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuma Iōjima|qq=}})]] '''Rotenburo''' (open-air bath) is a type of {{ill|Bath|ja|風呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bath|qq=}} built outdoors with no roof or enclosure[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82-663841 Kotobank - Digital Daijisen (Sanseidō)]. It is also called '''Noburo''' (野風呂) or '''Notenburo''' (野天風呂)When referred to as 野天風呂 (or 野風呂), it may mean a bath that is not entirely in its natural state like a {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Wilderness hot springs|qq=}} but lacks as much development in walls or roofing as a typical open-air bath.. == Overview == Open-air baths can be considered the original “landscape” of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen|qq=}} bathing. They began as naturally emerging hot springs like {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Wilderness hot springs|qq=}}, in which a simple bathing area (bathtub) was arranged. Rotenburo (open-air baths) are highly popular at hot spring resorts. Reasons include being able to enjoy scenic views while bathing, experiencing a sense of openness, and having less risk of overheating (since heat does not accumulate as much as in an indoor setting). As a result, many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|旅館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryokan|qq=}} and {{ill|Day-use hot spring facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-use hot spring facility|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip bathing facility|qq=}} offer open-air baths in addition to {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiyu|qq=}}. In recent years, not only traditional onsen but also many {{ill|Supersento|ja|スーパー銭湯|1=Super Sento|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Supersento|13=User:Immanuelle/Super Sento|qq=}} have installed open-air baths. Up through the early 1980s, indoor styles such as {{ill|Roman bath|ja|ローマ風呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Roman bath|qq=}} or {{ill|Jungle bath|ja|ジャングル風呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jungle bath|qq=}} were mainstream. However, around the late 1980s during the {{ill|Japanese asset price bubble|ja|バブル景気|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese asset price bubble|qq=}}, rotenburo began to draw more attention. Sometimes, even a bath that has a ceiling and three walls (thus feeling nearly as enclosed as an indoor bath) might be marketed as a “rotenburo,” or described as “semi-open-air” (“半露天”). == Rotenburo Banzuke == '''Rotenburo Banzuke''' (露天風呂番付) is a form of {{ill|Onsen banzuke|ja|温泉番付|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen banzuke|qq=}}, Ranking hot spring areas that are famous for their open-air baths. It was compiled in 1981, edited by '''Noguchi Fuyuto'''. In this Ranking, the Western {{ill|Yokozuna|ja|横綱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokozuna|qq=}} is the “Sunayu” at {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|qq=}}, and the Eastern Yokozuna is {{ill|Takaragawa Onsen|ja|宝川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaragawa Onsen|qq=}}. At Yubara Onsen, there is a large monument depicting this Rotenburo Banzuke, serving as a symbol of the hot spring resort. == Variations of Open-Air Baths == Yumura onsen20n4592.jpg|Open-air bath in a forest setting
({{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|2=ja|3=湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|4=ms|5=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|6=pl|7=Yumura Onsen|lt=Yumura Onsen|qq=draft|lt=Yumura Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|qq=}}) Ryokukeitouen Kirishima City Kagoshima Pref09n4592.jpg|Open-air bath at a mountain stream
({{ill|Sakaenoo Onsen|ja|栄之尾温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaenoo Onsen|qq=}}) 190714 Settuskyo Onsen Takatsuki Osaka pref Japan01s3.jpg|Open-air bath with a gorge view
({{ill|Settsukyō Onsen|ja|摂津峡温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsukyō Onsen|qq=}}) Alpa Komakusa 2008.jpg|Open-air bath with a lake view (Tazawako Kōgen Onsen “Alpa Komakusa”) Tamatsukuri onsen yado01s3648.jpg|A guest room with an attached open-air bath
({{ill|Tamatsukuri Onsen|ja|玉造温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsukuri Onsen|qq=}}) Koeda-Fudousan Katsura Koeda P6237036.JPG|A personal open-air bath built on private villa grounds == Management == [[File:新穂高の湯001.jpg|thumb|Cleaning an open-air bath
({{ill|Shinhodaka Onsen|ja|新穂高温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinhodaka Onsen|qq=}})]] From a management perspective, especially with a free-flowing hot spring of high source temperature, weather changes can significantly alter the bath’s temperature, requiring more precise temperature control. Additionally, due to the constant possibility of contamination by {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legionella|qq=}} bacteria, stricter hygiene measures than those for indoor baths are neededJapan Association of Living and Environmental Health “[http://www.seiei.or.jp/db-cho/le-11.html Legionella Prevention Guidelines]” (Revised March 1999; accessed January 5, 2016).. == Other == [[File:Iceland - Blue Lagoon 09 (6571266721).jpg|thumb|{{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|qq=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|qq=}} ({{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iceland|qq=}})]] * The open-air bath “Edo-jōwa Yoichi” at {{ill|Spa Resort Hawaiians|ja|スパリゾートハワイアンズ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spa Resort Hawaiians|qq=}} in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki, Fukushima|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}} is listed in the “{{ill|Guinness World Records|ja|ギネス世界記録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guinness World Records|qq=}} 2009” as the world’s largest open-air bath with a total tub area of 1000m²{{Cite web|和書|url=http://blog.hawaiians.co.jp/archives/50736632.html|title=世界一の大露天風呂|publisher=スパリゾートハワイアンズ|date=2008-11-08|accessdate=2021-06-03}}. * In {{ill|Iceland|ja|アイスランド|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iceland|qq=}}, there is an open-air spa called the {{ill|Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|ja|ブルーラグーン|qq=draft|lt=Blue Lagoon|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|qq=}} that covers about 5,000m² (with approximately 2,400m² open for general bathing)Iceland Tourism & Culture Research Institute “[http://www.iceland-kankobunka.jp/guide/reykjanes/blgn1.htm Tourist Spot Guide: Blue Lagoon]” (accessed January 5, 2016).. * {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26|qq=}} is “Open-Air Bath Day” (a pun on “Ro (6) Ten (.) Bu (2) Ro (6)”). It was established on June 26, 1987, by {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|qq=}} in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}Conceived in 1987 as part of a local revitalization project by young people in Yubara Onsen.[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html Official site of the 6.26 Open-Air Bath Day Committee] (Yubara Town Ryokan Cooperative). == Notes == == Sources == == See Also == * {{ill|part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburoでの組織的盗撮事件|qq=draft|1=Organized voyeurism case in an open-air bath|3=露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件|1=Organized Voyeurism Scandal at Rotenburo baths|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotenburo|qq=}} == External links == * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm Open-Air Bath Banzuke Chart] {{Onsen-stub}} '''Rotenburo''' (露天風呂) {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|露天風呂|version=102106783|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Bathing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Rotenburo]] [[ja:露天風呂]] [[ja:%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82]] [[ko:%EB%85%B8%EC%B2%9C%ED%83%95]]

{{afc comment|1={{ill|Sotoyu|ja|外湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotoyu|qq=}} refers to communal baths outside lodging facilities shared by multiple users, {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiyu|qq=}} denotes in-house hot spring baths privately installed within lodging facilities, and {{ill|Rotenburo|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotenburo|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotenburo|qq=}} describes open-air baths typically without a roof or full enclosure.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:41, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Onsen in Nachikatsuura, Japan.jpg|thumb|280px|An open-air bath overlooking the sea ([[:en:Nanki-Katsuura Onsen|Nanki-Katsuura Onsen]])]] [[File:Iwojima Higashi onsen.jpg|280px|thumb|A self-flowing hot spring “{{ill|Higashi Onsen (Iōjima)|ja|東温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Onsen (Iōjima)|qq=}}” facing the East China Sea ([[:en:Iōjima (Kagoshima)|Satsuma Iōjima]])]] '''Rotenburo''' (open-air bath) is a type of [[:en:Furo|Bath]] built outdoors with no roof or enclosure[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82-663841 Kotobank - Digital Daijisen (Sanseidō)]. It is also called '''Noburo''' (野風呂) or '''Notenburo''' (野天風呂)When referred to as 野天風呂 (or 野風呂), it may mean a bath that is not entirely in its natural state like a {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Wilderness hot springs|qq=}} but lacks as much development in walls or roofing as a typical open-air bath.. == Overview == Open-air baths can be considered the original “landscape” of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] bathing. They began as naturally emerging hot springs like {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Wilderness hot springs|qq=}}, in which a simple bathing area (bathtub) was arranged. Rotenburo (open-air baths) are highly popular at hot spring resorts. Reasons include being able to enjoy scenic views while bathing, experiencing a sense of openness, and having less risk of overheating (since heat does not accumulate as much as in an indoor setting). As a result, many [[:en:Ryokan|Ryokan]] and {{ill|Day-use hot spring facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-use hot spring facility|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip bathing facility|qq=}} offer open-air baths in addition to {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiyu|qq=}}. In recent years, not only traditional onsen but also many {{ill|Supersento|ja|スーパー銭湯|1=Super Sento|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Supersento|13=User:Immanuelle/Super Sento|qq=}} have installed open-air baths. Up through the early 1980s, indoor styles such as {{ill|Roman bath|ja|ローマ風呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Roman bath|qq=}} or {{ill|Jungle bath|ja|ジャングル風呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jungle bath|qq=}} were mainstream. However, around the late 1980s during the [[:en:Japanese asset price bubble|Japanese asset price bubble]], rotenburo began to draw more attention. Sometimes, even a bath that has a ceiling and three walls (thus feeling nearly as enclosed as an indoor bath) might be marketed as a “rotenburo,” or described as “semi-open-air” (“半露天”). == Rotenburo Banzuke == '''Rotenburo Banzuke''' (露天風呂番付) is a form of {{ill|Onsen banzuke|ja|温泉番付|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen banzuke|qq=}}, Ranking hot spring areas that are famous for their open-air baths. It was compiled in 1981, edited by '''Noguchi Fuyuto'''. In this Ranking, the Western {{ill|Yokozuna|ja|横綱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokozuna|qq=}} is the “Sunayu” at {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|qq=}}, and the Eastern Yokozuna is {{ill|Takaragawa Onsen|ja|宝川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaragawa Onsen|qq=}}. At Yubara Onsen, there is a large monument depicting this Rotenburo Banzuke, serving as a symbol of the hot spring resort. == Variations of Open-Air Baths == Yumura onsen20n4592.jpg|Open-air bath in a forest setting
([[:en:Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|Yumura Onsen]]) Ryokukeitouen Kirishima City Kagoshima Pref09n4592.jpg|Open-air bath at a mountain stream
({{ill|Sakaenoo Onsen|ja|栄之尾温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaenoo Onsen|qq=}}) 190714 Settuskyo Onsen Takatsuki Osaka pref Japan01s3.jpg|Open-air bath with a gorge view
({{ill|Settsukyō Onsen|ja|摂津峡温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsukyō Onsen|qq=}}) Alpa Komakusa 2008.jpg|Open-air bath with a lake view (Tazawako Kōgen Onsen “Alpa Komakusa”) Tamatsukuri onsen yado01s3648.jpg|A guest room with an attached open-air bath
([[:en:Tamatsukuri Onsen|Tamatsukuri Onsen]]) Koeda-Fudousan Katsura Koeda P6237036.JPG|A personal open-air bath built on private villa grounds == Management == [[File:新穂高の湯001.jpg|thumb|Cleaning an open-air bath
({{ill|Shinhodaka Onsen|ja|新穂高温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinhodaka Onsen|qq=}})]] From a management perspective, especially with a free-flowing hot spring of high source temperature, weather changes can significantly alter the bath’s temperature, requiring more precise temperature control. Additionally, due to the constant possibility of contamination by [[:en:Legionella|Legionella]] bacteria, stricter hygiene measures than those for indoor baths are neededJapan Association of Living and Environmental Health “[http://www.seiei.or.jp/db-cho/le-11.html Legionella Prevention Guidelines]” (Revised March 1999; accessed January 5, 2016).. == Other == [[File:Iceland - Blue Lagoon 09 (6571266721).jpg|thumb|[[:en:Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|Blue Lagoon]] ([[:en:Iceland|Iceland]])]] * The open-air bath “Edo-jōwa Yoichi” at [[:en:Spa Resort Hawaiians|Spa Resort Hawaiians]] in [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] is listed in the “[[:en:Guinness World Records|Guinness World Records]] 2009” as the world’s largest open-air bath with a total tub area of 1000m²{{Cite web|和書|url=http://blog.hawaiians.co.jp/archives/50736632.html|title=世界一の大露天風呂|publisher=スパリゾートハワイアンズ|date=2008-11-08|accessdate=2021-06-03}}. * In [[:en:Iceland|Iceland]], there is an open-air spa called the [[:en:Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|Blue Lagoon]] that covers about 5,000m² (with approximately 2,400m² open for general bathing)Iceland Tourism & Culture Research Institute “[http://www.iceland-kankobunka.jp/guide/reykjanes/blgn1.htm Tourist Spot Guide: Blue Lagoon]” (accessed January 5, 2016).. * [[:en:June 26|June 26]] is “Open-Air Bath Day” (a pun on “Ro (6) Ten (.) Bu (2) Ro (6)”). It was established on June 26, 1987, by {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|qq=}} in [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]Conceived in 1987 as part of a local revitalization project by young people in Yubara Onsen.[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html Official site of the 6.26 Open-Air Bath Day Committee] (Yubara Town Ryokan Cooperative). == Notes == == Sources == == See Also == * {{ill|part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburoでの組織的盗撮事件|qq=draft|1=Organized voyeurism case in an open-air bath|3=露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件|1=Organized Voyeurism Scandal at Rotenburo baths|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotenburo|qq=}} == External links == * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm Open-Air Bath Banzuke Chart] {{Onsen-stub}} '''Rotenburo''' (露天風呂) {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|露天風呂|version=102106783|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Bathing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Rotenburo]] [[ja:露天風呂]] [[ja:%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82]] [[ko:%EB%85%B8%EC%B2%9C%ED%83%95]]

{{afc comment|1={{ill|Sotoyu|ja|外湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotoyu|qq=}} refers to communal baths outside lodging facilities shared by multiple users, {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiyu|qq=}} denotes in-house hot spring baths privately installed within lodging facilities, and {{ill|Rotenburo|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotenburo|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotenburo|qq=}} describes open-air baths typically without a roof or full enclosure.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:41, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Onsen in Nachikatsuura, Japan.jpg|thumb|280px|An open-air bath overlooking the sea ([[:en:Nanki-Katsuura Onsen|Nanki-Katsuura Onsen]])]] [[File:Iwojima Higashi onsen.jpg|280px|thumb|A self-flowing hot spring “{{ill|Higashi Onsen (Iōjima)|ja|東温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Onsen (Iōjima)|qq=}}” facing the East China Sea ([[:en:Iōjima (Kagoshima)|Satsuma Iōjima]])]] '''Rotenburo''' (open-air bath) is a type of [[:en:Furo|Bath]] built outdoors with no roof or enclosure[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82-663841 Kotobank - Digital Daijisen (Sanseidō)]. It is also called '''Noburo''' (野風呂) or '''Notenburo''' (野天風呂)When referred to as 野天風呂 (or 野風呂), it may mean a bath that is not entirely in its natural state like a {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Wilderness hot springs|qq=}} but lacks as much development in walls or roofing as a typical open-air bath.. == Overview == Open-air baths can be considered the original “landscape” of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] bathing. They began as naturally emerging hot springs like {{ill|Wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wilderness hot springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Wilderness hot springs|qq=}}, in which a simple bathing area (bathtub) was arranged. Rotenburo (open-air baths) are highly popular at hot spring resorts. Reasons include being able to enjoy scenic views while bathing, experiencing a sense of openness, and having less risk of overheating (since heat does not accumulate as much as in an indoor setting). As a result, many [[:en:Ryokan|Ryokan]] and {{ill|Day-use hot spring facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-use hot spring facility|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip bathing facility|qq=}} offer open-air baths in addition to {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiyu|qq=}}. In recent years, not only traditional onsen but also many {{ill|Supersento|ja|スーパー銭湯|1=Super Sento|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Supersento|13=User:Immanuelle/Super Sento|qq=}} have installed open-air baths. Up through the early 1980s, indoor styles such as {{ill|Roman bath|ja|ローマ風呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Roman bath|qq=}} or {{ill|Jungle bath|ja|ジャングル風呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jungle bath|qq=}} were mainstream. However, around the late 1980s during the [[:en:Japanese asset price bubble|Japanese asset price bubble]], rotenburo began to draw more attention. Sometimes, even a bath that has a ceiling and three walls (thus feeling nearly as enclosed as an indoor bath) might be marketed as a “rotenburo,” or described as “semi-open-air” (“半露天”). == Rotenburo Banzuke == '''Rotenburo Banzuke''' (露天風呂番付) is a form of {{ill|Onsen banzuke|ja|温泉番付|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen banzuke|qq=}}, Ranking hot spring areas that are famous for their open-air baths. It was compiled in 1981, edited by '''Noguchi Fuyuto'''. In this Ranking, the Western {{ill|Yokozuna|ja|横綱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokozuna|qq=}} is the “Sunayu” at {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|qq=}}, and the Eastern Yokozuna is {{ill|Takaragawa Onsen|ja|宝川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaragawa Onsen|qq=}}. At Yubara Onsen, there is a large monument depicting this Rotenburo Banzuke, serving as a symbol of the hot spring resort. == Variations of Open-Air Baths == Yumura onsen20n4592.jpg|Open-air bath in a forest setting
([[:en:Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|Yumura Onsen]]) Ryokukeitouen Kirishima City Kagoshima Pref09n4592.jpg|Open-air bath at a mountain stream
({{ill|Sakaenoo Onsen|ja|栄之尾温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaenoo Onsen|qq=}}) 190714 Settuskyo Onsen Takatsuki Osaka pref Japan01s3.jpg|Open-air bath with a gorge view
({{ill|Settsukyō Onsen|ja|摂津峡温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsukyō Onsen|qq=}}) Alpa Komakusa 2008.jpg|Open-air bath with a lake view (Tazawako Kōgen Onsen “Alpa Komakusa”) Tamatsukuri onsen yado01s3648.jpg|A guest room with an attached open-air bath
([[:en:Tamatsukuri Onsen|Tamatsukuri Onsen]]) Koeda-Fudousan Katsura Koeda P6237036.JPG|A personal open-air bath built on private villa grounds == Management == [[File:新穂高の湯001.jpg|thumb|Cleaning an open-air bath
({{ill|Shinhodaka Onsen|ja|新穂高温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinhodaka Onsen|qq=}})]] From a management perspective, especially with a free-flowing hot spring of high source temperature, weather changes can significantly alter the bath’s temperature, requiring more precise temperature control. Additionally, due to the constant possibility of contamination by [[:en:Legionella|Legionella]] bacteria, stricter hygiene measures than those for indoor baths are neededJapan Association of Living and Environmental Health “[http://www.seiei.or.jp/db-cho/le-11.html Legionella Prevention Guidelines]” (Revised March 1999; accessed January 5, 2016).. == Other == [[File:Iceland - Blue Lagoon 09 (6571266721).jpg|thumb|[[:en:Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|Blue Lagoon]] ([[:en:Iceland|Iceland]])]] * The open-air bath “Edo-jōwa Yoichi” at [[:en:Spa Resort Hawaiians|Spa Resort Hawaiians]] in [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] is listed in the “[[:en:Guinness World Records|Guinness World Records]] 2009” as the world’s largest open-air bath with a total tub area of 1000m²{{Cite web|和書|url=http://blog.hawaiians.co.jp/archives/50736632.html|title=世界一の大露天風呂|publisher=スパリゾートハワイアンズ|date=2008-11-08|accessdate=2021-06-03}}. * In [[:en:Iceland|Iceland]], there is an open-air spa called the [[:en:Blue Lagoon (geothermal spa)|Blue Lagoon]] that covers about 5,000m² (with approximately 2,400m² open for general bathing)Iceland Tourism & Culture Research Institute “[http://www.iceland-kankobunka.jp/guide/reykjanes/blgn1.htm Tourist Spot Guide: Blue Lagoon]” (accessed January 5, 2016).. * [[:en:June 26|June 26]] is “Open-Air Bath Day” (a pun on “Ro (6) Ten (.) Bu (2) Ro (6)”). It was established on June 26, 1987, by {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|qq=}} in [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]Conceived in 1987 as part of a local revitalization project by young people in Yubara Onsen.[http://www.net626.co.jp/roten/index.html Official site of the 6.26 Open-Air Bath Day Committee] (Yubara Town Ryokan Cooperative). == Notes == == Sources == == See Also == * {{ill|part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburoでの組織的盗撮事件|qq=draft|1=Organized voyeurism case in an open-air bath|3=露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件|1=Organized Voyeurism Scandal at Rotenburo baths|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/part_of voyeurism in open-air baths incidents|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotenburo|qq=}} == External links == * [http://www.net626.co.jp/banduke.htm Open-Air Bath Banzuke Chart] {{Onsen-stub}} '''Rotenburo''' (露天風呂) {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|露天風呂|version=102106783|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Bathing]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Rotenburo]] [[ja:露天風呂]] [[ja:%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82]] [[ko:%EB%85%B8%EC%B2%9C%ED%83%95]] [[Category:Hot springs]]

{{afc comment|1={{ill|Sotoyu|ja|外湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotoyu|qq=}} refers to communal baths outside lodging facilities shared by multiple users, {{ill|Uchiyu|ja|内湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiyu|qq=}} denotes in-house hot spring baths privately installed within lodging facilities, and {{ill|Rotenburo|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotenburo|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotenburo|qq=}} describes open-air baths typically without a roof or full enclosure.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:41, 25 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Onsen in Nachikatsuura, Japan.jpg|thumb|280px|An open-air bath overlooking the sea ([[:en:Nanki-Katsuura Onsen|Nanki-Katsuura Onsen]])]] [[File:Iwojima Higashi onsen.jpg|280px|thumb|A self-flowing hot spring “{{ill|Higashi Onsen (Iōjima)|ja|東温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Onsen (Iōjima)|qq=}}” facing the East China Sea ([[:en:Iōjima (Kagoshima)|Satsuma Iōjima]])]] '''Rotenburo''' (open-air bath) is a type of [[:en:Furo|Bath]] built outdoors with no roof or enclosure[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%9C%B2%E5%A4%A9%E9%A2%A8%E5%91%82-663841 Kotobank - Digital Daijisen (Sanseidō)].
